system-page.name: index
system-page.title: Information Security Analyst Certificate
system-page.summary: Prepare for advanced roles in cybersecurity.
system-page.keywords: Information Security Analyst certificate, Networking Specialist Certificate, CompTIA Security+, Certified Ethical Hacker (CEH), CompTIA Cybersecurity Analyst (CyS)
system-page.description: Implement network security architecture and strategies to secure data and network infrastructure.

banner.alt: a server room

content.p: The %=ProgramTitle=% prepares you for advanced roles in cybersecurity. You'll learn to design, implement, and manage secure IT systems, perform vulnerability assessments, and respond effectively to cybersecurity threats and incidents as you prepare for the CompTIA Security+, Certified Ethical Hacker (CEH), and CompTIA Cybersecurity Analyst (CySA) certifications. Your education will equip you with the necessary skills and knowledge to design, implement, and manage secure IT systems, perform vulnerability assessments, and respond effectively to cybersecurity threats and incidents.

Stackable MCC certificates in this program include:
Help Desk Specialist Certificate > IT Support Specialist Certificate > Computer Network Support Specialist Certificate > Information Security Analyst Certificate building up to the Network Security AAS

a.data-acalog-program-name: Information Security Analyst Certificate, Sample Semester Pathway

breakdown.heading: What you need to know about getting your %=ProgramTitle=% (CIP %=CIP=%)

content.h3: What makes MCC's %=ProgramTitle=% program better than or distinct from similar programs at other institutions?
content.p: MCC’s program allows you to build on your skills by stacking the Help Desk Specialist Certificate, IT Support Specialist Certificate, Computer Network Support Specialist Certificate, and Information Security Analyst Certificate, earning credits that count toward your AAS degree.
content.h3: How will MCC's %=ProgramTitle=% program prepare me for my career or connect me to opportunities?
content.p: The program incorporates preparatory work for professional certification tests from CompTIA, Cisco, and Microsoft.

system-page.name: index
system-page.title: Heating, Ventilation, and Air Conditioning, AAS
system-page.summary: The HVAC AAS program at MCC offers comprehensive training in heating, ventilation, and air conditioning, preparing students for installation, maintenance, and repair of HVAC systems. Through hands-on work and real projects, students gain practical experience in refrigeration and electrical troubleshooting, boosting employability.
system-page.keywords: Heating, Ventilation, and Air Conditioning, CATI, HVAC degree, HVAC, refrigeration, cooling systems
system-page.description: Prepare for a career in Heating, Ventilation, and Air Conditioning.

banner.alt: a female technician looks at a monitor hooked up to an air conditioning unit

details.ProgramTitle: Heating, Ventilation, and Air Conditioning, AAS

content.p: Heating, ventilation, and air conditioning (HVAC) equipment is used in residential and commercial buildings every day, which means trained HVAC technicians who can install, maintain, and repair these complex systems are in high demand.

In MCC’s HVAC program, you’ll learn the skills you need to excel in this flexible, rewarding career. You’ll develop a comprehensive understanding of refrigeration, electrical troubleshooting, heating, air conditioning equipment, and sheet metal fabrication through hands-on classes taught by experts in the field. You’ll also gain real-world experience by working on projects in the surrounding community and learn how to install and service equipment using state-of-the-art diagnostic equipment.

Stackable MCC certificates include:
Residential Heating Certificate > Residential Air Conditioning Certificate > Electrical Troubleshooting Certificate > Residential Heating and Air Conditioning Installation Certificate, building up to the Heating, Ventilation, and Air Conditioning AAS

Career advancement certificates include:
Commercial Refrigeration Certificate, Commercial Heating I Certificate, Commercial Heating II Certificate, Commercial Air Conditioning I Certificate, and Commercial Air Conditioning II Certificate

Upgrade training certificates include:
Energy Audit Certificate, Sheet Metal Certificate

PATHWAY.TITLE: Heating, Ventilation, and Air Conditioning AAS

content.h3: What makes MCC's %=ProgramTitle=% program better than, or distinct from, similar programs at other institutions?

ul.li: Your MCC instructors worked with an advisory committee of local industry partners to develop a curriculum to teach the specific skills needed by area employers like Woodstock Heating and Cooling, Jensen's Plumbing and Heating Inc., Comfort Air Heating and Cooling, Inc., C and C Heating and Air Conditioning, Loud Brothers Heating Cooling Electric, American Comfort Central, Black Diamond Plumbing and Mechanical, McHenry Heating and Air Conditioning, Arctic Heating and Cooling, Algonquin Heating and Cooling, Epp's Custom Heating and Air, Master Tech Heating and Air Conditioning, V. M. O'Leary Sheet Metal and Heating, ABC Plumbing and Sewer and Heating and Cooling and Electric, and Elgin Sheet Metal.
The program’s service-learning approach allows you to gain real-world experiences while assisting others in the surrounding community.
The MCC Foglia Center for Advanced Technology and Innovation provides students with a state-of-the-art center for hands-on and practical learning with the latest tools, equipment, and HVAC systems.

content.h3: How will MCC's %=ProgramTitle=% program prepare me for my career or connect me to opportunities?

ul.li: You'll be prepared to earn EPA Section 608 Certification.
You'll gain hands-on experience in both residential and commercial HVAC systems.
Internships are available with local employers.

system-page.title: Residential Heating and Air Conditioning Installation Certificate
system-page.summary: Learn to install home heating and air conditioning equipment.
system-page.keywords: Heating, Ventilation, and Air Conditioning
system-page.description: Learn to install home heating and air conditioning equipment.

dynamic-metadata.name: metaTitle
dynamic-metadata.value: Residential Heating and AC Installation Certificate

banner.alt: A technician works on a roof-top chiller.

details.ProgramTitle: Residential Heating and Air Conditioning Installation Certificate

content.p: The %=ProgramTitle=% prepares entry-level heating, ventilation, and air conditioning (HVAC) technicians to install residential heating and air conditioning equipment. You will develop a basic understanding of window air conditioning units, split-system air conditioning units, air source heat pumps, geothermal heat pumps, gas heating systems, and electrical heating system installation. Training includes brazing and soldering torches, HVAC manifold gauge sets, refrigerant recovery equipment, vacuum pumps, and multimeter use.

Stackable MCC certificates include:
Residential Heating Certificate > Residential Air Conditioning Certificate > Electrical Troubleshooting Certificate > Residential Heating and Air Conditioning Installation Certificate building up to the Heating, Ventilation, and Air Conditioning AAS

Training includes sheet metal hand tools, bead rollers, sheet metal brakes, sheet metal shear, and Pittsburgh machine use.

system-page.name: index
system-page.title: Electrical Troubleshooting Certificate
system-page.keywords: Electrical systems, electrical troubleshooting, Heating, Ventilation, and Air Conditioning, CATI
system-page.description: Learn to troubleshoot electrical issues in home HVAC systems.

banner.alt: A technician works on a roof-top chiller.

content.p: The %=ProgramTitle=% prepares entry-level heating, ventilation, and air conditioning (HVAC) technicians for basic electrical troubleshooting on residential HVAC equipment. You will develop skills in reading wiring diagrams and schematics, and learn to use megohmmeters, voltmeters, ammeters, ohmmeters, and diagnostic test equipment. Training includes relays, contactors, solenoid valve coils, capacitors, and overload protection devices diagnosing, removal, and replacement.

Stackable MCC certificates include:
Residential Heating Certificate > Residential Air Conditioning Certificate > Electrical Troubleshooting Certificate > Residential Heating and Air Conditioning Installation Certificate building up to the Heating, Ventilation, and Air Conditioning AAS

content.h3: How will MCC's %=ProgramTitle=% program prepare me for my career or connect me to opportunities?

ul.li: You'll be prepared to earn EPA Section 608 Certification.

You'll gain hands-on experience with a variety of electrical and electronic systems used on both residential and commercial HVAC systems such as furnaces, air conditioning systems, refrigerators and freezers, reach-in coolers and freezers, case coolers and freezers, walk-in coolers and freezers, direct digital controls, and ice makers
You'll perform set-up and installation, servicing and cleaning, and diagnostics and repair of commercial HVAC refrigeration systems.
You'll develop skills in reading wiring diagrams and schematics, the use of megohmmeters, voltmeters, ammeters, ohmmeters, and diagnostic test equipment.
Internships are available with local employers.

system-page.name: index
system-page.title: Commercial Air Conditioning I Certificate
system-page.keywords: commercial, Heating, Ventilation, and Air Conditioning
system-page.description: Train for commercial air conditioning systems.

banner.alt: A technician works on a roof-top chiller.

content.p: The %=ProgramTitle=% prepares entry-level heating, ventilation, and air conditioning (HVAC) technicians to work on various commercial air conditioning systems. You'll develop a basic understanding of commercial split-system air conditioning units, air source heat pumps, geothermal heat pumps, rooftop air conditioning systems, direct digital controls, low-pressure chillers, and humidity control. Training includes electrical troubleshooting, the servicing and cleaning of commercial air conditioning systems, the use of brazing and soldering torches, HVAC manifold gauge sets, refrigerant recovery equipment, vacuum pumps, multimeters, and diagnostic test equipment.

system-page.name: index
system-page.title: Bookkeeping Certificate
system-page.summary: Prepare to get a job as an accounts payable clerk, accounts receivable clerk, bookkeeper, or teller.
system-page.teaser: Earn your Bookkeeping Certificate and learn how to analyze, record, and summarize accounting transactions manually, in QuickBooks®, and using Peachtree® General Ledger.
system-page.keywords: Bookkeeping Certificate, bookkeeping, accounts payable clerk, accounts receivable clerk, bookkeeper, teller, book keeping, book-keeping, book keeper, book-keeper
system-page.description: Learn how to analyze, record, and summarize accounting transactions manually, in QuickBooks®, and using Peachtree® General Ledger.

banner.alt: accountant with journal and calculator

content.p: You have a good head for numbers. Maybe you've helped out a local business as an unofficial bookkeeper and you're considering a career change. Earn your %=ProgramTitle=% and learn how to analyze, record, and summarize accounting transactions manually, in QuickBooks® and using Peachtree® General Ledger.
As a bookkeeper, you'll be responsible for general accounting functions that include:

ul.li: preparing general journal entries
maintaining balance sheet schedules and ledgers and bank reconciliations
assisting with monthly costing and account analysis

breakdown.heading: What you need to know about getting your %=ProgramTitle=% (CIP %=CIP=%)

content.h3: What makes MCC's %=ProgramTitle=% program better than or distinct from similar programs at other institutions?

ul.li: MCC's accounting programs are designed to be stackable. A stackable career track provides a pathway for advancement through a career ladder. The pathways are clearly designed sequences of career-related courses. You can accumulate a sequence of certificates over time to build on your qualifications and to help you to advance along a career path. Stackable credentials assist you in advancing in your career, providing for higher-paying jobs, which can lead to a higher standard of living.

content.h3: What job opportunities are available to someone with a %=ProgramTitle=%?
content.p: Prepare to work as an accounts payable clerk, accounts receivable clerk, bookkeeper, or teller or as an entry-level accountant in business administration, operations, or a financial operations department.

section.h3: How will MCC's %=ProgramTitle=% prepare me for my career or connect me to opportunities?

ul.li: Ask about internships with local businesses

section.h3: What have graduates gone on to do?

ul.li: Many students start as bookkeepers and continue on to get Tax Practitioner Certificates and their AAS in Accounting before earning their CPAs
Several students have opened their own businesses
Take advantage of free accounting tutoring through the Tutoring and Academic Support Center

system-page.name: index
system-page.title: Tax Practitioner Certificate
system-page.summary: MCC's accounting department offers a Tax Practitioner Certificate to prepare for your Enrolled Agent (EA) Examination.
system-page.keywords: Enrolled Agent, EA Exam, Accounting, accounting certificate, Tax Practitioner
system-page.description: Prepare for your Enrolled Agent (EA) Examination.

banner.alt: accountant with journal and calculator

content.p: The %=ProgramTitle=% consists of four tax courses: individual, corporate, Internal Revenue Service (IRS) practice and procedures and the Enrolled Agent Review course or Volunteer Income Tax Assistance (VITA) Tax Clinic participation. These four courses are spread out throughout the calendar year enabling you to prepare for your Enrolled Agent (EA) Examination.

An EA is a federally-authorized tax practitioner with technical expertise in the field of taxation, who is empowered by the U.S. Department of the Treasury to represent taxpayers before all administrative levels of the IRS for audits, collections, and appeals. As an EA, you'll prepare and/or review tax returns and represent taxpayers before the IRS and state governments.

content.h3: What makes MCC's %=ProgramTitle=% program better than or distinct from similar programs at other institutions?

li.span: MCC pioneered the %=ProgramTitle=% program that is now being duplicated at other institutions and by the National Association of Enrolled Agents.
MCC's program delivers content beyond what is necessary for you to successfully pass the Enrolled Agent (EA) exam, offering intense training on taxpayer representation.

content.h3: What job opportunities are available to someone with a %=ProgramTitle=%?

content.h3: How will MCC's %=ProgramTitle=% program prepare me for my career or connect me to opportunities?

become successfully self-employed
be invited to sit on boards of not-for-profit entities

content.p: The %=ProgramTitle=% is tailored for those who wish to pursue a career in a medical area, such as medical administrative assistant, medical secretary, or medical office assistant. Learn keyboarding, medical terminology, human biology, communication skills, and more.

system-page.name: index
system-page.title: Paralegal Certificate
system-page.summary: Earn your Paralegal Studies Certificate in as little as 18 months!
system-page.keywords: paralegal certificate, paralegal certificate, paralegal degree, paralegal certificate program, paralegal training, paralegal courses
system-page.description: Earn your Paralegal Studies Certificate in as little as 18 months!

banner.alt: close up of gavel and striking block with scale

content.p: Earn your %=ProgramTitle=% in as little as 18 months! This program is ideal for those desiring to work in the legal field, planning to attend law school after earning their bachelor’s degree, or looking to change careers. An associate degree is not required before pursuing the certificate. You can also enter the program immediately after high school graduation, or, with MCC’s Running Start Program, you can begin taking paralegal courses while still in high school. (Check with your high school counselor or home school official for eligibility requirements).

If you have strong research, writing, and organizational skills or are interested in going into the legal field and possibly becoming a lawyer, becoming a paralegal is a great career choice! Paralegals work under the direction of a lawyer making decisions that call for understanding basic legal concepts, managing information, and preparing documents in specialized areas of the law. Typical job duties of a paralegal include:

ul.li: Interviewing clients and potential witnesses
Preparing trial evidence
Maintaining case files
Summarizing depositions
Researching legal issues
Writing briefs, interoffice memos, contracts, interrogatories, and letters

AREA.value: Marketing, Finance, Business Management and Administration

a.data-acalog-program-name: Paralegal Studies Certificate, Sample Semester Pathway

breakdown.heading: What you need to know about getting your %=ProgramTitle=% (CIP %=CIP=%)

content.h3: What makes MCC's %=ProgramTitle=% program better than or distinct from similar programs at other institutions?
content.p: MCC’s program is specifically tailored for the paralegal market in Chicagoland and McHenry County. With the help and guidance of our advisory board, made up of lawyers, judges, paralegals, and professors, we have designed a unique program with an emphasis on the needs of the existing legal community. You’ll focus on class subjects that are the most sought-after and applicable within our local markets.
content.h3: What job opportunities are available to someone with a %=ProgramTitle=%?
content.p: You'll be qualified to work as a paralegal in law offices, State and Federal Court systems, and for non-profits and corporations.
content.h3: How will MCC's %=ProgramTitle=% program prepare me for my career or connect me to opportunities?
content.p: Our mandatory internship program provides you with practical experience as you network and develop a portfolio of written work. Your internship may even lead to employment opportunities.
content.h3: By the Numbers:

ul.li: The salary range within a 50-mile radius of MCC for a paralegal is $30,000 for part-time to over $110,000 full-time employment.
In 2015, there were 67 job listings for paralegals within a 25-mile radius of MCC and 526 listings within a 50-mile radius.
In 2013, there were 12,390 paralegals and legal assistants in Illinois, and the number of positions is projected to increase 17.52% by 2022 to 14,022. (ides.illinois.gov)

content.h3: Outside the Classroom

li.a: The American Association for Paralegal Education

content.h3: Paralegal-Specific Career Information

li.a: US Department of Labor - Bureau of Labor and Statistics

system-page.name: index
system-page.title: Architectural and Engineering Design Technology Certificate
system-page.summary: The Architectural and Engineering Design certificate serves students seeking to enter the drafting and design field as well as those looking to develop their skills and advance in their careers.
system-page.keywords: Architectural and Engineering Design Technology Certificate, Computer Aided Design, CAD, drafting
system-page.description: Prepare to enter the drafting and design field or develop your skills and advance in your career.

banner.alt: Architectural Engineering Technology

details.ProgramTitle: Architectural and Engineering Design Technology Certificate

content.p: Almost everything built or made today starts with computer drawings. Designers and drafters create these technical drawings for manufacturing and construction.

The %=ProgramTitle=%, with multiple specialization options, represents a collective approach to developing or upgrading the necessary visualization, graphical communication, and organization skills required of this ever-evolving field. CAD technicians with the requisite technical knowledge, visual talent, and organizational skills can expect to find work in such diverse fields as mechanical/architectural design, civil engineering, surveying, commercial art, and interior design. This certificate program is for you, whether you're just starting in drafting and design or looking to boost your skills and advance your career.

section.heading: What makes MCC's Architectural and Engineering Design Technology Certificate program better than or distinct from similar programs at other institutions?

content.p: MCC offers your choice of three specialization options:

ol.li: Engineering Technology Specialization Option
Architectural Technology Specialization Option
Interior Design Specialization Option

content.p: The AET certificate is offered in conjunction with the Fast Track. The Fast Track is a cohort-based program. Through the Fast Track, you can earn a certificate in two years or less.

MCC has experienced instructors who have proven themselves in the field and a newly renovated design center that features Angle-CAD design workstations, AutoCAD software, AutoDesk Inventor 3D software and new computers.

banner.alt: woman making a purchase at a farmer's market
banner.videoTitle: locally grown food featured at MCC and Dukes Alehouse

details.ProgramTitle: Entrepreneurial Agriculture Business and Marketing Certificate

content.p: If you've worked on a farm, or if you own your own farm, but need record keeping, analysis, and marketing expertise to make your business viable, the %=ProgramTitle=% was designed for you.

If you have extensive gardening experience but are not familiar with small- to mid-scale commercial production, consider MCC's Entrepreneurial Agriculture Production Certificate or the Entrepreneurial Agriculture AAS.
After completing the %=ProgramTitle=%, you'll be able to:

ul.li: Build a strategic business plan that includes income and expense for a farm business launch, a marketing plan, and three- to five-year projections
Market farm products with confidence
Develop useful record-keeping systems for a food/farm business

content.p: This certificate program is offered in collaboration with the Center for Agrarian Learning.

banner.alt: automotive students working under hood of car
banner.videoTitle: about the Automotive Maintenance Tech Certificate

content.p: Many automotive repair facilities are experiencing a shortage of qualified entry-level technicians. Our %=ProgramTitle=% program is designed to train you for these entry-level positions. You can complete the program in three semesters by attending courses part-time, two days per week.

The %=ProgramTitle=% allows you to complete a technical education program, join the workforce, and return to school later to complete your Advanced Automotive Technician Certificate or Automotive Technology AAS.

system-page.name: index
system-page.title: Business Management, AAS
system-page.summary: MCC's Business Management AAS combines business, management, accounting, and marketing courses to prepare you for entry-level and mid-level management positions.
system-page.keywords: managers, business managers, Business Management, career skills, Organizational Leadership, Organizational Leadership Certificate, Business Management Principles Certificate, International Business Certificate, Entrepreneurship Certificate, Entrepreneurship, ENT
system-page.description: Prepare for entry-level and mid-level management positions as you learn how to read a balance sheet, the legal implications of contracts, and more.

banner.alt: business manager reviewing data

content.p: MCC's %=ProgramTitle=% combines business, management, accounting, and marketing courses. Prepare for entry-level and mid-level management positions as you learn how to read a balance sheet, the legal implications of contracts, and managers' roles in business today. Courses involve discussion, case studies, business simulations, and application-based projects that prepare you to enter the workforce in virtually any business.

Already a manager? The program can also help those already in management positions fine-tune their skills and become more effective in decision-making, problem-solving, and human relations – three vital areas for business managers.

AREA.value: Marketing, Finance, Business Management and Administration

breakdown.heading: What you need to know about getting your %=ProgramTitle=% (CIP %=CIP=%)

content.h3: How will MCC's %=ProgramTitle=% program prepare me for my career or connect me to opportunities?
content.p: Classroom computer simulations will help you learn how to run a company and compete in the marketplace.

This program is designed to be stackable. You can accumulate certificates over time to build on your qualifications and help you advance along a career path. Credits from your Business Management Principles Certificate and Organizational Leadership Certificate apply to your Business Management AAS.
content.h3: Outside of the Classroom

ul.li: Participate in Business Club
Ask about internship opportunities

div.p: The mission of the Business Department is to provide a high-quality learning environment that promotes entrepreneurial thinking, creativity, problem-solving, communication, and soft skills in achieving their career goals.
Students will:

ol.li: Demonstrate critical thinking and decision-making skills through the application of discipline-specific concepts to business issues.
Appropriately use discipline-specific terminology in verbal and written form.
Explain ethical behavior norms in business.
Display professional etiquette and behavior.
Utilize resources and other technology effectively and efficiently.

banner.alt: entrepreneur looking over data spreadsheets

content.p: Are you starting, owning, managing, or expanding a business? MCC's %=ProgramTitle=% offers some of the state's most comprehensive and convenient entrepreneurship training.

The program will prepare you to start your own business and/or help manage a small business, and you’ll develop a business plan to help you succeed and seek funding. Learn from actual small-business owners and managers who have real-world experience to help you focus your studies on the most current and relevant topics today.

You'll learn from actual small-business owners and managers who have real-world experience to help you focus your studies on the most current and relevant topics today.

ul.li: Open and manage a photography studio
Own and operate a senior healthcare service company

system-page.title: International Business Studies Certificate
system-page.keywords: International Business Studies, IBS
system-page.description: Take courses in topics ranging from the legalities of international business to exporting and from business in Japan to international marketing.

banner.alt: view company data on tablet

p.strong: This program is accredited by the Association of Collegiate Business Schools and Programs.

content.p: This specialty %=ProgramTitle=% is designed to provide both owners and employees of businesses with the necessary information in the international marketplace to effectively compete. Students who want to broaden their business education beyond traditional courses will also benefit from this program.
Enrollment may be in an individual course, or courses may be applied toward a certificate.

Note: International Business courses are offered in conjunction with William Rainey Harper College through a joint partnership. Harper College and MCC students will register with their respective colleges, even if the online course they are taking is at the other college.

system-page.name: index
system-page.title: Beverage Management Certificate
system-page.teaser: Learn how to plan a successful beverage operation, including regulatory requirements, employee training, and merchandising of beverages.
system-page.keywords: beverage, wine, culinary, beer
system-page.description: Learn how to plan a successful beverage operation.

banner.alt: alt text

content.p: Take your culinary arts career to the next level by learning how to plan a successful beverage operation, including regulatory requirements, employee training, and merchandising of beverages. Examine the history of the craft beer industry, basic beer making, types, styles, and distributors. Immerse yourself in the history and service techniques of the wine industry.
After completing your %=ProgramTitle=% you'll be able to:

ul.li: Explain the history of wine culture and wine making
Demonstrate how to taste and select alcohol for a variety of menus properly
Develop a bar layout and create a cocktail and beverage menu to complement food
Design tasting menus and food pairings for a variety of alcohol choices

breakdown.heading: What you need to know about getting your %=ProgramTitle=% (CIP %=CIP=%)

content.h3: What makes MCC's %=ProgramTitle=% program better than, or distinct from, similar programs at other institutions?

ul.li: MCC has worked closely with restaurant owners, managers, and hospitality-related businesses in the district to develop a curriculum to address the type of skill sets that they need to develop to fill vacant positions. These advisory board members have consistently advised of the need for advanced alcohol service training and hospitality management training.
Many of the classes required for this certificate are stackable into our culinary or pastry AAS degrees.
The college has partnered with Woodstock North High School and Crystal Lake South High School to offer dual credit opportunities where appropriate.

The certificate program includes experience in the back and front of the house for baking and culinary disciplines. MCC is one of the few culinary programs to have both a student-run bakery and a restaurant.

Our culinary certificates lead to transfer opportunities in hospitality management programs that offer bachelor’s degrees.
MCC has articulation agreements for hospitality programs with Southern Illinois University and guaranteed admission agreements with Northern Illinois University, and Roosevelt University.

content.h3: What job opportunities are available to someone with an %=ProgramTitle=%?
content.p: The Chicagoland metropolitan area supports, and is expected to continue to support, the need for a significant number of people in hospitality-related careers.
content.h3: Outside the Classroom
content.p: In addition to standard coursework, you have the opportunity to enroll in a working classroom via Tartan Bistro, our student-run restaurant, and Sweet Scots, our student-run bakery. With department chair permission, you may also apply to be paid interns in either the student-run bakery or restaurant.

system-page.name: index
system-page.title: Baking and Pastry Assistant I Certificate
system-page.summary: Build a strong foundation for your career in the baking and pastry industries by earning your Baking and Pastry Assistant I Certificate.
system-page.keywords: Baking and Pastry Assistant I Certificate, culinary, chef, pastry, baking
system-page.description: Prepare for entry-level baking and pastry positions.

banner.alt: pastries on display at a bakery

details.ProgramTitle: Baking and Pastry Assistant I Certificate

content.p: The %=ProgramTitle=% builds a strong foundation that allows you to further specialize as you progress in your career in the baking and pastry industries. This certificate program focuses on sustainable food preparation, handling, sanitation, and safety, as well as business and management principles. You'll gain the knowledge and hands-on skills needed for entry-level positions, including (but not limited to): baker or pastry chef assistant, prep baker, scaler, decorator, dough laminator, cake baker, cookie baker, and similar positions in institutional food service, small to large hospitality operations, cruise ships and more.

Stackable MCC certificates in this program include: Baking and Pastry I Certificate > Baking and Pastry II Certificate > Assistant Restaurant Manager Certificate building up to the Baking and Pastry Management AAS

iframe.title: tour of our state of the art culinary classroom

p.span: Take a virtual tour of our state-of-the-art culinary teaching lab.

breakdown.heading: What you need to know about getting your %=ProgramTitle=% (CIP %=CIP=%)

content.h3: What makes MCC's %=ProgramTitle=% program better than or distinct from similar programs at other institutions?
content.p: Complete your Baking and Pastry Assistant I Certificate while working toward your Baking and Pastry Management AAS or Culinary Management AAS at MCC.
Be sure to apply for MCC scholarships to help defray the cost of your education. We have several scholarships for culinary students.
content.h3: How will MCC's Baking and Pastry Assistant I Certificate program prepare me for my career or connect me to opportunities?
content.p: The curriculum is designed to meet industry standards set by the American Culinary Federation (ACF) as well as the National Restaurant Association Educational Foundation (NRAEF).
The NRAEF prepares students for the NRAEF ManageFirst® industry certification as well as the ServSafe® Sanitation Manager certification.

In addition to standard coursework, you have the opportunity to enroll in a working classroom via Tartan Bistro, our student-run restaurant, and Sweet Scots, our student-run bakery. With department chair permission, you may also apply to be paid interns in either the student-run bakery or restaurant.

You'll study sustainable food preparation, handling, sanitation, and safety, as well as business and management principles. This course of study gives you the knowledge and hands-on skills needed for entry-level positions, including (but not limited to): line cook, line lead cook, commis, chef assistant, prep cook, soup or sauce cook, and similar positions in institutional food service, small to large hospitality operations, cruise ships and more.

content.h3: What makes MCC's %=ProgramTitle=% program better than or distinct from similar programs at other institutions?
content.p: The program is designed so you can complete your Chef's Assistant I Certificate or Baking and Pastry Assistant I Certificate while working toward your Culinary Management AAS or your Baking and Pastry Management AAS.
Be sure to apply for MCC scholarships to help defray the cost of your education. We have several scholarships for culinary students.
content.h3: How will MCC's %=ProgramTitle=% program prepare me for my career or connect me to opportunities?
content.p: The curriculum is designed to meet industry standards set by the American Culinary Federation (ACF) and the National Restaurant Association Educational Foundation (NRAEF).
The NRAEF prepares students for the NRAEF ManageFirst® industry certification and the ServSafe® Sanitation Manager certification.
content.h3: Outside of the Classroom
content.p: In addition to standard coursework, you have the opportunity to enroll in a working classroom via Tartan Bistro, our student-run restaurant, and Sweet Scots, our student-run bakery. With department chair permission, you may also apply to be paid interns in either the student-run bakery or restaurant.

system-page.name: index
system-page.title: AAS in Mobile Design and Development
system-page.summary: Apply the credits from your Android Development Certificate, iOS Development, and Programming Fundamentals certificates toward your Mobile Design and Development AAS degree.
system-page.keywords: Application development, app development, mobile application development, Computer Information Systems, AAS, Computer Technology, web development
system-page.description: Learn to design and develop applications for mobile phones.

banner.alt: app development

details.ProgramTitle: AAS in Mobile Design and Development

content.p: Can't live without your smartphone? Have an idea for an app to make your life easier? Earn your Mobile Design and Development AAS degree and learn to design and develop mobile applications! We provide a comprehensive education for students with no experience, as well as an excellent opportunity for people changing careers or looking to take individual courses to stay current in their existing jobs.

Today's application development includes so much more than games. Businesses of all types are clamoring for ways to engage with their mobile customers. There are many high-paying, in-demand jobs for mobile application developers, but employers expect to see formal education on applicants’ resumes. Start by earning a certificate in Android Development, IOS Development, or Programming Fundamentals and apply those credits toward your AAS degree in Mobile Design and Development.
Once you've learned design theory and have experience in programming for multiple mobile platforms, consider rounding out your skillset with our Web Design and Development AAS program.

content.h3: What makes MCC's %=ProgramTitle=% program better than or distinct from similar programs at other institutions?

ul.li: MCC has gathered an advisory team of business leaders to guide development of this program to ensure that the curriculum prepares you for today's job market.
By offering critical-thinking exercises, presentations and real-world experience, we help you develop the necessary technical and soft skills employers are looking for.
The program’s labs run the latest software for both Android and iOS devices.

div.p: The mission of McHenry County College’s Mobile Design and Development Program is to develop students who are skilled in analyzing business needs, managing resources, and implementing mobile solutions. MCC students shall be distinguishable by their critical thinking, effective communication, professionalism, and technical competency. Students will use current and contemporary practices to demonstrate mastery in the Program Learning Outcomes.

ol.li: Analyze business web development needs and develop mobile solutions for those needs.
Select technologies appropriate to implement for business needs.
Demonstrate proficiency in a variety of current mobile development tools.
Select appropriate resources based upon legal guidelines for licensing and fair use.
Integrate multiple technologies into a comprehensive business solution.
Implement effective project management to handle deadlines, communication, and budgets.

div.p: Degree: Mobile Design and Development Degree
Certificates: Programming Fundamentals, Android Development, iOS Development

system-page.name: index
system-page.title: Android Development with Integrated AI Certificate
system-page.summary: Learn the fundamentals of programming in the Android Studio Development Environment using the Java language and core Java concepts and language fundamentals as they apply to Android app development.
system-page.keywords: Android, Android Studio Development, app development, application, mobile app
system-page.description: Learn the fundamentals of programming in the Android Studio Development Environment.

banner.alt: app developers collaborating

details.ProgramTitle: Android Development with Integrated AI Certificate

content.p: Dive into Android development with integrated AI to create intelligent, user-friendly apps ready for the Google Play Store. The %=ProgramTitle=% program focuses on designing apps with Artificial Intelligence (AI) features like voice interactions and personalized recommendations, utilizing tools like TensorFlow Lite and ML Kit. A key element is learning data visualization to present AI insights effectively with libraries such as MPAndroidChart. You’ll develop and rigorously test your app using AI-powered tools like GitHub Copilot and then deploy it with a strong App Store Optimization strategy. Continuous improvement post-launch, through performance monitoring and staying updated with the latest AI trends, ensures your app remains competitive and innovative.
The key concepts you’ll learn in this certificate program will prepare you to pursue your career or build upon your education to earn an Associate in Applied Science degree.

a.data-acalog-program-name: Android with Integrated AI Certificate, Sample Semester Pathway

content.h3: What makes MCC's %=ProgramTitle=% program better than or distinct from similar programs at other institutions?

ul.li: Credits earned in this certificate are stackable towards other AOT certificates and the AOT AAS degree (see above), helping you achieve your educational goals faster, and earning multiple credentials as you go.
Students completing this certificate will complete the Word, Excel, Outlook, and PowerPoint MOS certification exams - nationally recognized credentials.
Many AOT courses have reduced or zero textbook costs, with more courses added each academic year.
Classes include course projects common to industry.
You may participate in Scots for Non-Profits, volunteering at a local non-profit organization, toward earning graduation Silver Cord hours.
You'll learn from educated and experienced faculty who will share their valuable experience, knowledge, and troubleshooting skills with you.

content.h3: How will MCC's %=ProgramTitle=% program prepare me for my career or connect me to opportunities?
content.p: You will learn using current, industry-standard software (Microsoft 365) and train to the current standards in the field and towards certificate exams.
Ask about internships for college credit and job shadowing opportunities!
content.h3: What have graduates gone on to do?
content.p: Students completing this certificate have retooled or refined their skills towards a promotion or advancing or changing careers. Many students have continued with AOT classes towards additional AOT certificates and the AOT AAS degree. Some students have secured a part-time or “stepping-stone” position after certificate completion while working towards the degree.
Other students have completed this certificate purely to obtain their MOS certifications to improve or enhance their skills and knowledge toward excelling in their careers.
content.h3: Outside of the Classroom
content.p: To further your career, you may wish to seek national certification credentials beyond MCC’s certificate and/or degree. Organizations offering certification and/or professional resources for administrative professionals include:

system-page.name: index
system-page.title: Emergency Medical Technician Certificate
system-page.summary: MCC provides specialized emergency medical technician courses for those interested in becoming professionally trained emergency medical personnel.
system-page.keywords: EMS, EMT, paramedics, Emergency Medical Technicians, ambulance, paramedic
system-page.description: Training for future firefighters, paramedics, or EMTs.

banner.alt: ems students in emergency treatment scenario

content.p: In MCC's hands-on %=ProgramTitle=% program, you'll learn how to identify and treat injuries and illnesses. Spend time in the classroom, practice skills in a lab, and gain real-world experience in a hospital emergency department. Pass the National Registry EMT competency exam after the course to get your EMT license in Illinois and start your career!

Working as an EMS provider requires significant physical strength, dexterity, and the ability to function in very adverse environments with exposure to numerous safety risks typically found at emergency scenes. Can you meet or exceed these EMS Provider Functional Requirements and Physical Expectations?
MCC also offers you the opportunity to cross-train through our Fire Science program.

AREA.value: Law, Public Safety, Corrections, and Security

breakdown.heading: What you need to know about getting your %=ProgramTitle=% (CIP %=CIP=%)

content.h3: What makes MCC's %=ProgramTitle=% program better than or distinct from similar programs at other institutions?
content.p: MCC's EMS program provides opportunities for a range of students and working professionals in this field.

dl.dd: This is the entry level-course that provides the non-medical responder with knowledge and basic skills to provide first response treatment to victims of illness and injury with the goal to maintain basic life functions until an ambulance/rescue unit arrives.

An EMT provides a detailed study of patient assessment and basic life support management of someone experiencing illness or traumatic injuries. The goal of treatment is to recognize abnormal physical assessment findings and provide basic life support treatments. Upon successful completion of the classroom and clinical requirements for the program, students are eligible for licensure examination within the state of Illinois. Licensure is required for employment.

The paramedic training program provides an intensive study of the human body with focus on the recognition of life threatening medical illnesses and traumatic injuries. The goal of patient care is to deliver advanced life support treatments based on assessment findings. Upon successful completion of the classroom, lab and clinical internship requirements, students are eligible for licensure examination within the state of Illinois. Licensure is required for employment.

link.label: Ambulance Drivers and Attendants, Except Emergency Medical Technicians

content.h3: How will MCC's %=ProgramTitle=% program prepare me for my career or connect me to opportunities?
content.p: All EMS faculty are professionals with multiple years of working in the EMS field in the McHenry County community. You'll benefit as much from your instructor's experience and hands-on skills labs as you will from the formal curriculum. Many instructors invite other licensed professionals into the classroom to share their perspectives, giving you insight into the day-to-day realities of the field.
content.h3: What have graduates gone on to do?
content.p: Graduates of our EMS program have gone on to work for the Secret Service, Federal Emergency Management Agency, coroner's office, and local healthcare providers like Northwestern.
content.h3: Outside of the Classroom

ul.li: National Association of Emergency Medical Technicians (NAEMT)

system-page.name: index
system-page.title: Exercise Specialist Certificate
system-page.summary: The Exercise Specialist Certificate program prepares students for careers in personal training, exercise training, and life coaching within one year. Participants can earn national certifications from organizations like ACSM and NSCA, with a high success rate in passing exams upon completion of the coursework. The program includes hands-on experience through internships and is connected to local employers for job opportunities after graduation.
system-page.keywords: Exercise Specialist, Exercise Specialist Certificate, personal training, exercise training, strength and conditioning, Fitness Instructor Training, personal trainer, fitness instructor, fitness trainers, aerobics instructors, recreation and fitness studies
system-page.description: Earn your Exercise Specialist Certificate in three semesters! Prepare for a career in personal training with hands-on experience and accredited coursework.

banner.alt: girl in gym doing floor exercises

content.p: The %=ProgramTitle=% prepares you for a career in personal training, exercise training, strength and conditioning, or life coaching if you do not wish to pursue a two-year Associate's degree and/or you already have a degree and want to get certified as a personal trainer in one year.

You'll study material from the American College of Sports Medicine (ACSM), the National Strength and Conditioning Association (NSCA), the National Academy of Sports Medicine (NASM), and other national governing bodies so that as you complete your coursework, you'll be preparing for your choice of national certifications. Most of our students complete one or more of these certifications by the time their coursework is finished. In fact, 90 to 100% of our students successfully pass a national certification exam.
You will take the required internship for this program in the summer after completing all other course requirements, completing this credential in a total of three semesters.
If you want to earn a degree, our %=ProgramTitle=% courses can be applied to your Kinesiology and Health Studies, AAS.

a.data-acalog-program-name: Exercise Specialist Certificate, Sample Semester Pathway

content.p: We work with local employers who serve as fieldwork sites. Many employers hire our students after their fieldwork internships.
content.h3: What have graduates gone on to do?
content.p: Some of our students have been hired by area fitness centers and training facilities, such as Good Shepherd/Advocate, Barrington Park District, Dave Davis Speed Center, Centegra Health Systems, and Del Webb Sun City.
content.h3: Outside of the Classroom
content.p: Learn through hands-on experience in our on-site Fitness Center and during your fieldwork internship.

system-page.name: index
system-page.title: Paramedic Certificate
system-page.summary: When earning your Emergency Medical Technician - Paramedic Certificate, you receive 17 hours of college credit and a certificate from the Illinois Department of Public Health to practice in the state of Illinois as a licensed paramedic.
system-page.keywords: Emergency Medical Technician - Paramedic Certificate, EMS, EMT, paramedics, Emergency Medical Technicians, ambulance, paramedic, Cindy Amore
system-page.description: This training is considered advanced emergency care and can be a stand-alone profession or can be linked to a fire service career.

banner.alt: ems students in emergency treatment scenario

content.p: The %=ProgramTitle=% includes four paramedic modules and one internship semester. You will earn 38 hours of college credit and be eligible to sit for the National Registry of Emergency Medical Technicians Exam. A successful attempt at the NREMT exam allows the student to apply for a professional license from all 50 states. This training is considered advanced emergency medical care and can be used to obtain jobs in EMS, the fire service, hospital systems, and private industry.

Working as an EMS provider requires significant physical strength, dexterity, and the ability to function in very adverse environments with exposure to numerous safety risks typically found at emergency scenes. Can you meet or exceed these EMS Provider Functional Requirements and Physical Expectations?
content.h3: CAAHEP Accreditation Statements
content.p: The McHenry County College Paramedic program is accredited by the Commission on Accreditation of Allied Health Education Programs ( www.caahep.org) upon the recommendation of the Committee on Accreditation of Educational Programs for the Emergency Medical Services Professions (CoAEMSP).

To contact CAAHEP
Commission on Accreditation of Allied Health Education Programs
25400 U.S. Highway 19 North, Suite 158
Clearwater, FL 33763
www.caahep.org

To contact COAEMSP
Committee on Accreditation of Educational Programs for the Emergency Medical Services Professions
8301 Lakeview Parkway, Suite 111-312
Rowlett TX 75088
(214) 703-8445
FAX (214) 703-8992
www.coaemsp.org
content.h3: Credit for Experiential Learning Disclaimer
content.p: Due to licensing regulations, the McHenry County College Paramedic program does not recognize credit for experiential learning.

In addition, you'll learn how to select, prepare, and handle fresh, dried, and artificial floral materials and how to successfully manage a retail floral shop. Your workday responsibilities in this field may include filling floral design orders and assisting in the planning of events such as weddings, funerals, and holiday events with individuals.

system-page.title: Advanced Engineering Technician Certificate
system-page.summary: The Advanced Engineering Technician Certificate program is designed to prepare you for careers in manufacturing and engineering technology, addressing the growing demand for skilled technicians.
system-page.keywords: advanced engineering technician certificate, manufacturing technology, CNC machining, automation, skilled manufacturing technicians, industrial engineering
system-page.description: Prepare for careers in manufacturing and engineering technology.

banner.alt: closeup of male in a hard hat pushing buttons on a CNC machine

content.p: McHenry County is one of the fastest-growing manufacturing sectors in the region, accounting for more than 25 percent of the county's earnings. There's a high demand for skilled manufacturing technicians within McHenry County and nationwide. Manufacturing design and engineering technicians play a crucial role in the product design process and development. As companies demand more versatile workers, career opportunities will continue to grow in all areas of manufacturing technology.

The %=ProgramTitle=% Program prepares you to work in the field of engineering and technology, specializing in industrial and manufacturing engineering and problem-solving. The program is designed to meet the growing need created by the technology revolution for college-educated problem solvers who can support the engineering process.
Choose the option that best suits your educational and professional goals.

content.p: Earn your CNC Machining Certificate and learn skills that are directly applicable in the operation of computer-controlled machines performing one or more machine functions on metal or plastic workpieces. Become familiar with Computer Numerically Controlled (CNC) programming using G codes and conversational programming.

CNC Machine Tool Programmers, Metal and Plastic develop programs to control machining or processing of metal or plastic parts by automatic machine tools, equipment, or systems. Job titles include CNC programmer, process engineer, programmer, project engineer, software engineer, welding engineer, CAD/CAM programmer.

system-page.name: index
system-page.title: Industrial Maintenance Technician Certificate
system-page.summary: Develop an understanding of industrial machines and how they operate as you earn your Industrial Maintenance Technician Certificate at MCC.
system-page.keywords: Industrial Maintenance, Industrial Maintenance Program, CNC, advanced manufacturing, CAD, electronics, robotics, hydraulics, pneumatics, programmable logic controls
system-page.description: Develop an understanding of industrial machines and how they operate.

banner.alt: engineering student working with manufacturing equipment

content.p: Are you a problem solver? Do you like working with your hands? Build on your knowledge and skills to earn your %=ProgramTitle=%.

Maintenance technicians are multi-skilled individuals proficient in many tasks that allow them to assemble, install, troubleshoot, repair, and modify machinery and automated systems. You might work with computer or electronically-controlled equipment in both manufacturing and facilities environments.

Every day is different as you identify problems and determine the best and safest means of resolving the issue. This certificate will give you a strong foundation and knowledge of a variety of pumps, motors, motor controls, mechanical drives, preventive/predictive maintenance, hydraulics, pneumatics, and related safety concepts. People who are mechanically inclined, skilled at working with tools, and have excellent hand-to-eye coordination often find the greatest success in this type of role.

a.data-acalog-program-name: Industrial Maintenance Technician Certificate, Sample Semester Pathway

breakdown.heading: What you need to know about getting your %=ProgramTitle=% (CIP %=CIP=%)

content.h3: What makes MCC's %=ProgramTitle=% program better than or distinct from similar programs at other institutions?

ul.li: MCC has worked closely with local manufacturers to develop a curriculum to address the skill sets they need to develop to fill vacant positions. In addition, local manufacturers provide some faculty and content experts to teach courses in this program.
Learn about combining job-related credit courses with structured on-the-job training through an apprenticeship!

content.h3: How will MCC's %=ProgramTitle=% program prepare me for my career or connect me to opportunities?

ul.li: MCC is offering Engineering Technology AAS-registered apprenticeships with specializations in CNC Machining Professional and Industrial Maintenance Technician

As part of IMT135, Maintenance Management, you'll develop and implement preventative maintenance programs within the manufacturing environment. These projects create real-world simulations of what you can expect to find when working in the manufacturing environment.

content.h3: Outside of the Classroom
content.p: You'll have the opportunity to tour local manufacturing plants to understand better the scheduling and the impact that maintenance has on the production floor.

The classes required for this certificate are stackable into our Industrial Management and Technology AAS or Business Management AAS degrees.

You'll also have opportunities to network with industry professionals within the college as well as business leaders as part of the prescribed coursework and internship opportunities.
Take advantage of MCC's Career Services and the resources available for career information, resume building, and job search activities.

content.h3: What have graduates gone on to do?
content.p: The courses within this program are great preparation for American Production and Inventory Control Society (APICS) certification, an industry standard within the manufacturing and supply chain management sectors. Companies look for that knowledge base when selecting new candidates for hire and after completing this program, you'll be poised for success from the onset with this skillset.

system-page.name: index
system-page.title: Basic Nursing Assistant Certificate
system-page.summary: After you successfully complete the Basic Nursing Assistant Certificate Program, you are eligible to take the written State Nursing Aide Competency Exam. When you pass the Nurse Aide Competency Exam, your name is added to the Healthcare Worker Registry, and you can be employed as a Certified Nursing Assistant (CNA).
system-page.teaser: The nursing assistant education course (basic nursing assistant certificate program) is designed to prepare nursing assistants to work under the direction of the licensed professional nurse to provide care in various health care settings. After you successfully complete the Basic Nursing Assistant Certificate Program, you are eligible to take the written State Nursing Aide Competency Exam. When you pass the Nurse Aide Competency Exam, your name is added to the Healthcare Worker Registry, and you can be employed as a Certified Nursing Assistant (CNA).
system-page.keywords: cna, Certified Nursing Assistant, CNA, Nursing Assistant Education, CNA, nursing, nursing program, nursing information session schedule, healthcare, health
system-page.description: After you successfully complete the Basic Nursing Assistant Certificate Program, you are eligible to take the written State Nursing Aide Competency Exam.

banner.alt: close up of MCC nursing patch

content.p: This seven-credit-hour course is designed to prepare nursing assistants to work under the direction of a licensed professional nurse to provide care in various healthcare settings. Coursework includes the development of basic nursing skills through lectures, laboratory demonstration and practice, and 48 hours of clinical experience. The Illinois Department of Public Health has approved the course for a total of 136 hours of instruction.

After you successfully complete the %=ProgramTitle=% Program, you are eligible to take the written State Nursing Aide Competency Exam. When you pass the Nurse Aide Competency Exam, your name is added to the Healthcare Worker Registry, and you can be employed as a Certified Nursing Assistant (CNA).
An individual will be placed on the Nurse Aide Registry when:

ul.li: s/he has successfully completed a training program approved in accordance with the Long-Term Care Assistants and Aides Training Program Code (77 Ill. Adm.Code 395)

And, s/he has met the required criminal background check information (Section 300.661), and there are no findings of abuse, neglect, or misappropriation of property in accordance with Sections 3-206.01 and 3-206.02 of the Nursing Home Act. Disqualifying Convictions in accordance with the Health Care Worker Background Check Act (225 ILCS 46).

content.h3: Requirements and Deadlines

ul.li: Note: Early registration deadline - The last day to register is May 12.
Fingerprinting is on May 21 at MCC. Details will be sent to your MCC student email.
Note: Early registration deadline - The last day to register is August 4.
The Health Care Worker Background Check form is due August 6. Check your MCC student email on August 5 to complete it online.
Fingerprinting is on August 13 at MCC. Details will be sent to your MCC student email.
Note: Early registration deadline - The last day to register is January 5.
The Health Care Worker Background Check form is due January 7. Check your MCC student email on January 6 to complete it online.
Fingerprinting is on January 14 at MCC. Details will be sent to your MCC student email.

li.a: Basic Nursing Assistant Admission Process and Guidelines
Requirements and deadlines due before class
Medical form for physical and TB test
Basic Nursing Assistant Frequently Asked Questions

breakdown.heading: What you need to know about getting your %=ProgramTitle=% (CIP %=CIP=%)

content.h3: What makes MCC's %=ProgramTitle=% program better than, or distinct from, similar programs at other institutions?

ul.li: The State of Illinois requires a nursing assistant program to be 120 hours. MCC's program offers 136 hours, allowing students to gain more competency in their skills.

content.h3: How will MCC's %=ProgramTitle=% program prepare me for my career or connect me to opportunities?

ul.li: Many universities require 2,000 patient contact hours before admission in their physician assistant programs. Some medical schools are beginning to require patient contact hours too. CNA certification provides this patient contact and may assist you in earning income as you work toward your degree.
An applicant to MCC's Nursing AAS program must be a CNA with documentation of good standing on the Illinois Health Care Worker registry.
MCC offers a Clinical Medical Assistant Program that requires students to be in good standing on the Illinois Health Care Worker registry.
MCC offers ECG technician training designed for students who have completed their CNA.

content.h3: What have graduates gone on to do?

ul.li: Students have gone on to become medical technologists, and work in hospitals, clinics, physicians' offices, and skilled nursing facilities.

content.h3: Outside of the Classroom

ul.li: This course includes theory, lab practice, and a clinical component. Working with patients in a clinical setting teaches you how to work and communicate with people.

div.p: The mission of McHenry County College’s Basic Nursing Assistant Certificate Program is to develop students who are skilled to perform competently in the role of a certified nursing assistant (CNA). MCC students shall be distinguishable by their critical thinking, effective communication, professionalism, and technical competency. Students will use current and contemporary practices to demonstrate mastery of the Program Learning Outcomes.
Students will:

ol.li: Demonstrate proficiency in the 21 plus required skills per IDPH guidelines.
Think critically and act appropriately in health care settings.
Communicate clearly and appropriately in health care settings.
Develop an awareness of, and commitment to, ethical behavior in health care settings.

breakdown.heading: What you need to know about getting your %=ProgramTitle=% (CIP %=CIP=%)

content.h3: What makes MCC's %=ProgramTitle=% program better than, or distinct from, similar programs at other institutions?

ul.li: MCC has worked closely with local marketers and other business owners in the district to develop a curriculum to address the type of skill sets that they need to develop to fill vacant positions in McHenry County.
MCC partners with district 528 high schools to provide dual credit courses and a career pathway to the Small Business Marketing Certificate at MCC.
Students who earn their Small Business Marketing Certificate can continue to complete their Associate in Applied Science in Marketing or Associate in Applied Science in Business Management.

content.h3: What job opportunities are available to someone with an %=ProgramTitle=%?
content.p: The Chicagoland metropolitan area supports, and is expected to continue to support, the need for a significant number of people with Small Business Marketing skills.

ul.li: Marketing Assistant
Social Media Specialist
Marketing Coordinator
Marketing and Advertising Sales Representative

content.h3: How will MCC's %=ProgramTitle=% program prepare me for my career or connect me to opportunities?

ul.li: You'll benefit from hands-on experience running a marketing campaigns for small business establishments, as well as managing and measuring outcomes. You'll also develop a business plan that includes a comprehensive marketing plan.
Your instructors, industry experts in business, marketing and management, will discuss obtaining positions in marketing as part of the curriculum.

content.h3: By the Numbers:

ul.li: Nationally, the U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics projects employment for marketing manager occupations to grow by 10% by 2026 ( Bureau of Labor Statistics, U.S. Department of Labor)
Potential salaries for marketing-related positions range from $28,000 to $73,000 depending on the type of position.

system-page.name: index
system-page.title: Social Media Marketing Certificate
system-page.keywords: Social Media Marketing Certificate, marketing, social media, digital marketing, business management
system-page.description: Prepare for an entry-level position that requires marketing skills or a four-year degree in Marketing, Social Media Marketing, and/or Business Management.

banner.alt: students using social media marketing

content.p: It's easy to be entertaining on Instagram or Twitter but, can you effectively communicate or influence consumer behavior via social media? How do you analyze and measure the success of your efforts? Social media platforms change but the principles of social media strategy stay the same. As you earn your %=ProgramTitle=%, you'll examine strategic uses of social media as part of a company's marketing approach and become versed in various social media tools, campaign development, and social media marketing analytics. The Social Media Marketing Certificate prepares you for an entry-level position that requires marketing skills or a four-year degree in Marketing, Social Media Marketing, and/or Business Management.

There are no pre-requisite courses for the %=ProgramTitle=% introductory courses so, whether you're in high school, adult education, or workforce training, you can easily begin working toward your %=ProgramTitle=%!

AREA.value: Marketing, Finance, Business Management and Administration

Students who earn their Social Media Marketing Certificate can continue on to complete their Associate in Applied Science in Marketing or Associate in Applied Science in Business Management.

content.h3: By the Numbers:

ul.li: Nationally, the U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics projects employment for marketing manager occupations to grow by 10% by  2026 ( Bureau of Labor Statistics, U.S. Department of Labor)
Potential salaries for marketing-related positions range from $28,000 to $73,000 depending on the type of position.

system-page.name: index
system-page.title: Advanced Welding Technician Certificate
system-page.summary: The Advanced Welding Technician Certificate prepares you for a more advanced-level career in production welding as well as small fabrication shops and the construction industry.
system-page.keywords: welding, TIG, MIG
system-page.description: Prepare for a more advanced-level career in production welding, small fabrication shops, and the construction industry.

banner.alt: woman welding student wearing helmet

content.p: The %=ProgramTitle=% gives you the skills to push beyond production welding, helping you find a career in custom fabrication, job shop work, or plant maintenance. Complete the Basic Welding Technician Certificate and then choose from applicable welding courses in Shielded Metal Arc Welding (SMAW), Gas Tungsten Arc Welding (GTAW), and Fabrication. The %=ProgramTitle=% allows you to become eligible for specialized welding certifications through the American Welding Society (AWS).

a.data-acalog-program-name: Advanced Welding Technician Certificate, Sample Semester Pathway

breakdown.heading: What you need to know about getting your %=ProgramTitle=% (CIP %=CIP=%)

content.h3: What makes MCC's %=ProgramTitle=% program better than, or distinct from, similar programs at other institutions?

ul.li: MCC has worked closely with local manufacturers and employers in the welding industry to ensure our courses provide the skills local employers are looking for.
MCC’s curriculum includes a GMAW Pulse Spray Transfer course, which is requested by many local employers seeking entry-level welders.
Our welding certificates complement the credit-based skills in MCC’s industrial maintenance programs.

content.h3: How will MCC's %=ProgramTitle=% program prepare me for my career or connect me to opportunities?

ul.li: Industry partners needing welders have reviewed and approved the curriculum, ensuring you'll learn the skills they need today. These industry partners meet with MCC annually to review the curriculum, discuss industry standards and any related changes, and offer advice for keeping graduates employment-ready in this field.

Course material offered throughout the welding certificates is designed to help you develop the requisite skills to pass the certified welder tests in accordance with the American Welding Society standards. The final projects in the capstone course in each certificate program are similar to those required for you to be certified in American Welding Society D1.1 Structural Welding Steel.
Courses are taught by industry experts in welding who will discuss obtaining positions in welding as part of the curriculum.

link.label: Welding, Soldering, and Brazing Machine Setters, Operators, and Tenders

breakdown.heading: What you need to know about getting your %=ProgramTitle=% (CIP %=CIP=%)

content.h3: What makes MCC's %=ProgramTitle=% program better than or distinct from similar programs at other institutions?
content.p: Apply for MCC scholarships to help defray the cost of your education. We have several scholarships for culinary students.
content.h3: How will MCC's %=ProgramTitle=% program prepare me for my career or connect me to opportunities?
content.p: The curriculum is designed to meet industry standards set by the American Culinary Federation (ACF) as well as the National Restaurant Association Educational Foundation (NRAEF).
The NRAEF prepares students for the NRAEF ManageFirst® industry certification as well as the ServSafe® Sanitation Manager certification.
content.h3: Outside of the Classroom
content.p: In addition to standard coursework, you have the opportunity to enroll in a working classroom via Tartan Bistro, our student-run restaurant, and Sweet Scots, our student-run bakery. With department chair permission, you may also apply to be paid interns in either the student-run bakery or restaurant.

The %=ProgramTitle=% program introduces you to the fundamental principles of horticulture, delving into topics like plant biology, plant selection, and landscape management. You'll be exposed to various horticultural tools and equipment as you develop skills in propagation, plant identification, and landscape maintenance.

system-page.title: Policing Certificate
system-page.summary: Learn the fundamental terms, concepts, and laws of criminal justice, as well as the essential role played by police in the justice system.
system-page.keywords: Police, Policing, Criminal Justice, Criminal Justice AAS, law enforcement
system-page.description: Learn about the essential role police play in the justice system.

banner.alt: criminal justice in wood letters

content.p: Do you find yourself seeking out true-crime podcasts and documentaries? Are you interested in serving and protecting others? If so, MCC’s Policing Certificate may be a perfect fit for you!

As an introductory certificate, this program helps you develop skills in police science and learn the basic concepts of criminal justice, criminal law, and community policing. You’ll gain the foundation necessary to enter the field of criminal justice and get a first look into this rewarding and challenging career. The certificate is also stackable, meaning it counts toward the Criminal Justice AAS degree at MCC if you choose to continue your studies.

breakdown.heading: What you need to know about getting your %=ProgramTitle=% (CIP %=CIP=%)

content.h3: What makes MCC's %=ProgramTitle=% better than or distinct from similar programs at other institutions?

ul.li: All classes are taught by local, real-world practitioners in the field. Our academic classes are instructed by master's degree-educated professionals who are either retired or still working.
We educate students on the academic discipline while incorporating real field experience.
Our classes go further and incorporate guest speakers and trips that feature actual McHenry County area subject matter experts and facilities.

div.p: The mission of McHenry County College’s Criminal Justice Program is to develop students who are skilled in the basic concepts and terminology they will need to intelligently analyze the process of American Criminal Justice as it unfolds daily across the country. MCC students shall be distinguishable by their critical thinking, effective communication, professionalism, and technical competency. Students will use current and contemporary practices to demonstrate mastery of the Program Learning Outcomes. Students who graduate from the criminal justice program may work in law enforcement as a police officer, school resource officer, evidence technician, a community service officer, or may work in the court system or correctional system. Though the AAS is not designed as a transfer degree, students may also choose to pursue a bachelor’s degree after completing the AAS in Criminal Justice.

ol.li: Students will be able to analyze information about the criminal justice system as measured by effective study and research.
Students will be able to demonstrate the ability to think critically and be open to new ideas and perspectives as exhibited in classroom discussions.
Students will be able to demonstrate an awareness of and commitment to ethical behavior in criminal justice as measured by examination and written reports.
Students will use written and verbal communication skills necessary to work in the criminal justice community as measured by the presentation of research papers.

link.label: Criminal Justice and Law Enforcement Teachers, Postsecondary

system-page.name: index
system-page.title: Criminal Justice Certificate
system-page.summary: Learn the fundamental terms, concepts, and laws of criminal justice.
system-page.keywords: Criminal Justice Certificate, Criminal Justice, Criminal Justice AAS, law enforcement, judicial process, corrections, criminology, community policing
system-page.description: Learn the fundamental terms, concepts, and laws of criminal justice.

banner.alt: criminal justice in wood letters

content.p: MCC’s %=ProgramTitle=% develops students who are skilled in the basic concepts and terminology needed to analyze the process of American criminal justice as it unfolds daily across the country. Classes introduce ethical critical thinking skills and provide training for various public policy situations. You’ll also learn about the administrative side of the criminal justice system as well as substantive and procedural criminal law.

Classes address topics such as the judicial process, corrections, criminology, and community policing. The program provides the foundation for you to begin an exciting and rewarding career in criminal justice. The certificate is also stackable, meaning it counts toward the Criminal Justice AAS degree at MCC if you choose to continue your studies. It is also a good fit if you're currently working in the criminal justice field and looking to advance in your career.

system-page.summary: MCC’s Basic Welding Technician Certificate will prepare you for an entry-level career in welding. MCC has worked closely with local manufacturers and employers in the welding industry to ensure our courses provide the skills local employers are looking for.

content.p: MCC’s %=ProgramTitle=% will prepare you for an entry-level position in welding. You'll learn welding safety, and blueprint reading, and will have experience with Gas Metal Arc Welding (GMAW), Flux Cored Arc Welding (FCAW), Shielded Metal Arc Welding (SMAW), and Gas Tungsten Arc Welding (GTAW) processes. You'll be eligible for specialized welding certifications through the American Welding Society (AWS).

ul.li: MCC has worked closely with local manufacturers and employers in the welding industry to ensure our courses provide the skills local employers are looking for.
MCC’s curriculum includes a GMAW Pulse Spray Transfer course, which is requested by many local employers seeking entry-level welders.

There are no pre-requisite courses for the %=ProgramTitle=% introductory courses, so students in high school, adult education, or other workforce training programs can easily transition to the college to take these courses. Those with prior welding coursework may transfer it to the college for advanced placement in welding courses.
Our welding certificates complement the credit-based skills in MCC’s manufacturing and industrial maintenance programs.
