system-page.name: index
system-page.title: Accounting, AAS
system-page.summary: MCC’s accounting program lays a strong foundation for a variety of positions—specifically for employment with accounting and bookkeeping firms, business organizations, and governmental agencies.
system-page.keywords: Accounting, accounting degree, accounting AAS, accounting certificate, Tax Practitioner, career program
system-page.description: Prepare for employment with accounting and bookkeeping firms, business organizations, and governmental agencies.

banner.alt: accountant with journal and calculator

content.p: Accountants compile and analyze business records and prepare financial reports, such as profit and loss statements, balance sheets, cost studies, and tax reports. As accounting procedures in business and industry sectors become more and more complicated, the job outlook for accountants is very promising.

MCC’s Accounting program develops students who are skilled for entry-level positions in both the public and private sectors of the accounting and business community. The program teaches accounting theory, and practical skills, and provides internship opportunities to students in both supportive and challenging classroom environments.

Start with a Bookkeeping, Tax Practitioner, or Accounting Certificate and build toward your AAS degree. Graduates of the program can pursue accounting positions with the opportunity to advance to a junior accountant level. Accountants who continue their education can pursue unlimited opportunities to excel in business.

breakdown.heading: What you need to know about getting your %=ProgramTitle=% (CIP %=CIP=%)

content.h3: What makes MCC's Accounting AAS program better than, or distinct from, similar programs at other institutions?

ul.li: MCC's accounting programs are designed to be stackable. A stackable career track provides a pathway for advancement through a career ladder. The pathways are clearly-designed sequences of career-related courses. You can accumulate a sequence of certificates over time to build on your qualifications and to help you to advance along a career path. Stackable credentials assist you in advancing in your career, providing for higher paying jobs, which can lead to a higher standard of living.
You'll learn from full-time faculty with over 50 combined years of teaching experience and adjunct instructors working in the accounting field today.

link.label: Tax Examiners, Collectors, and Revenue Agents

content.h3: What job opportunities are available to someone with an %=ProgramTitle=%?

ul.li: Junior Accountant
Staff Accountant
Tax Accountant
Financial Services Analyst

content.h3: What have graduates gone on to do?

ul.li: senior manager at Ernst & Young
many students transfer to four-year colleges and sit for the CPA Exam
many choose to work locally in accounting firms, manufacturing companies, and financial advisory firms

content.h3: Outside the Classroom

ul.li: Take advantage of free accounting tutoring through the Tutoring and Academic Support Center

li.a: Participate in MCC's VITA Tax Clinic

ul.li: Take part in MCC's annual High School Accounting Bowl

li.a: American Institute of Certified Public Accountants

div.p: The mission of McHenry County College’s Accounting Program is to develop students who are skilled for entry-level positions in both the public and private sectors of the accounting and business community. The Accounting program teaches accounting theory and practical skills and provides internship opportunities to students in supportive and challenging classroom environments.
Students will:

ul.li: Receive and record accounting source documents for accounts payable, accounts receivable, and payroll,
Analyze check figures, postings, and documents for correct entry, mathematical accuracy, and proper coding,
Operate spreadsheet and word processing software to perform calculations and produce documents, for internal and external users,
Operate computers programmed with accounting software to record, store, analyze information, and produce financial statements,
Comply with federal, state, and company policies, procedures, and regulations.

spotlight.name: jill-scot
spotlight.title: Jill Scot
spotlight.keywords: Jill Scott, Spotlight, Alumni Spotlight, MCC Alumni, alum
spotlight.description: She was inspired to pursue an anthropology career path after taking her first anthropology course -- Introduction to Anthropology -- with Judi Cameron, Ph.D. at MCC.

system-page.name: index
system-page.title: Accounting Certificate
system-page.summary: Start with an accounting, bookkeeping, or tax practitioner certificate and build toward your AAS degree.
system-page.keywords: Accounting, accounting certificate, Tax Practitioner
system-page.description: Prepare to be a junior accountant in business, government, or industry.

banner.alt: accountant with journal and calculator

content.p: There is an acute shortage of persons with the training needed to be successful in accounting. The %=ProgramTitle=% prepares you to be a junior accountant in business, government, and industry. Job opportunities exist in accounting firms, manufacturing companies, financial institutions, insurance companies, service companies, not-for-profit, and governmental entities.
As a junior accountant, you may:

ul.li: Support senior accountants in carrying out the responsibilities of an accounting department

Be responsible for general accounting functions that include preparing journal entries; maintaining balance sheet schedules and ledgers and bank reconciliations; and assisting with monthly closing and account analysis

breakdown.heading: What you need to know about getting your %=ProgramTitle=% (CIP %=CIP=%)

content.h3: What makes MCC's Accounting Certificate program better than or distinct from similar programs at other institutions?

ul.li: MCC's accounting programs are designed to be stackable. A stackable career track provides a pathway for advancement through a career ladder. The pathways are clearly-designed sequences of career-related courses. You can accumulate a sequence of certificates over time to build on your qualifications and to help you to advance along a career path. Stackable credentials assist you in advancing in your career, providing for higher paying jobs, which can lead to a higher standard of living.
You'll learn from full-time faculty with over 50 combined years of teaching experience and adjunct instructors working the accounting field today.

content.h3: How will MCC's Accounting Certificate prepare me for my career or connect me to opportunities?

ul.li: MCC students have had accounting internships with the following local businesses: True Value Barrington, Aptar, Sage, Follett, Centegra,  Silgan Tubes, Brown Printing, Walmart in Johnsburg, TC Industries, Countryside, McHenry County Federal Credit Union, Baxter Credit Union, American Community Bank, Home State Bank, Scot Forge, AJE Associates, Exemplar Financial, Wells Fargo Financial, Sam’s Club, Gary Lang Auto Group, and  Round the Clock Restaurant.
Many students go on to become CPAs and work in the local communities.

content.h3: What have graduates gone on to do?

ul.li: Several graduates work at Exemplar Financial in an accounting capacity
Several graduates worked as interns at AptarGroup, a worldwide manufacturing corporation with headquarters in Crystal Lake

content.h3: Outside the Classroom

ul.li: Take advantage of free accounting tutoring through the Tutoring and Academic Support Center
Take part in MCC's annual High School Accounting Bowl

li.a: American Institute of Certified Public Accountants

system-page.name: index
system-page.title: Software Application Design and Development, AAS
system-page.summary: The Software Application Design and Development program offers an Associate's Degree to equip students to design and develop software for web, mobile, and desktop platforms.
system-page.keywords: Application development, app development, mobile application development, Computer Information Systems, AAS, Computer Technology, web development
system-page.description: Learn to design and develop web, computer, and mobile device software.

banner.alt: two young males look at a computer monitor

details.ProgramTitle: Software Application Design and Development, AAS

content.p: Combine your creativity and computer know-how with a rewarding career in a growing industry.

MCC’s degree and certificate programs in Software Design and Development teach the essentials of front-end and back-end development, combining programming, database fundamentals, and UI/UX design. You’ll learn to code in multiple languages, including HTML, CSS, JavaScript, and Python, and gain the skills to keep up with the latest software development tools, even after you graduate.

Beyond the technical aspect of the curriculum, you’ll learn how to address business requirements of software design, test applications, and communicate effectively with stakeholders. In your capstone course, you’ll also get a chance to apply your skills by working on projects for a local business or creating a professional-grade personal project.

MCC’s program allows you to build on your skills by stacking the Back-End Development, Front-End Development, and Web Developer Certificates, earning credits that count toward your AAS degree. Additional training is available in standalone certificates in iOS, Android, and Python, which are also specialty subsets of the degree.

Faculty with industry experience help you explore career paths and be better prepared to enter the workforce.

system-page.name: index
system-page.title: Welding and Fabrication, AAS
system-page.summary: Prepare for careers in areas such as production welding, equipment manufacturing, general fabrication, construction, or custom fabrication.
system-page.keywords: Welding and Fabrication AAS, welding, metal fabrication, forging, AWS Level 1—Entry Welder, American Welding Society (AWS) certification
system-page.description: Prepare for careers in the welding and metalworking industry.

banner.alt: Welding student in class wearing welding helmet

content.p: Prepare for a successful career in the field of welding and metalworking. In MCC’s Welding program, you’ll gain extensive experience in welding safety and the major welding processes: Shielded Metal Arc Welding (SMAW), Gas Metal Arc Welding (GMAW), Flux Cored Arc Welding (FCAW), and Gas Tungsten Arc Welding (GTAW). You’ll also develop print reading, metal forming, machining, and fabrication skills by working on various industry-level projects.

McHenry County has one of the fastest-growing manufacturing sectors in the region, and many of these manufacturers seek those with skills in heavy-duty metal fabrication and forging. Graduates leave well-prepared for careers in production welding, equipment manufacturing, general fabrication, construction, or custom fabrication. The AAS degree also allows students to become eligible for specialized welding certifications through the American Welding Society.

Stackable MCC certificates include:
Manufacturing Essentials Certificate > Pre-Apprenticeship Welding Technician Certificate > Production Welding Technician Certificate > Basic Welding Technician Certificate > Advanced Welding Technician Certificate building up to the Welding and Fabrication AAS

Career advancement certificates include:
Pre-Apprenticeship Welding Technician Certificate

a.data-acalog-program-name: Welding and Fabrication, AAS Sample Semester Pathway

breakdown.heading: What you need to know about getting your %=ProgramTitle=% (CIP %=CIP=%)

content.h3: What makes MCC's %=ProgramTitle=% program better than, or distinct from, similar programs at other institutions?

ul.li: MCC has worked closely with local manufacturers and welding-related employers in the district to develop a curriculum to address the type of skill sets that they need to develop to fill vacant positions.
MCC’s curriculum includes a GMAW Pulse Spray Transfer course, which is requested by many local employers seeking entry-level welders.

There are no prerequisite courses for the Welding Technician Certificate introductory courses, so students in high school, adult education, or other workforce training programs can easily transition to the college to take these courses. Those with prior welding coursework may transfer it to the college for advanced placement in welding courses.
Our welding certificates complement the credit-based skills in MCC’s manufacturing and industrial maintenance programs.

content.h3: How will MCC's %=ProgramTitle=% program prepare me for my career or connect me to opportunities?
content.p: The %=ProgramTitle=% program uses a predominantly hands-on approach to train you in the skills needed to be successful in the workforce. The program is project-based, incorporating several team-based projects. You get to work as a team, simulating the work conditions you'll encounter on the job. The joint capstone course has you working with students from other areas of study in a collaborative project requiring expertise from machinists, welders, and automation technicians. You'll create complex machine assemblies utilizing teamwork, skills learned throughout the program, and input from industry professionals.

div.p: The mission of McHenry County College's Welding and Fabrication Program is to prepare you for a successful career in the field of welding and fabrication. You will gain extensive experience in welding safety and the major welding processes: Shielded Metal Arc Welding (SMAW), Gas Metal Arc Welding (GMAW), Flux Cored Arc Welding (FCAW), and Gas Tungsten Arc Welding (GTAW). You will develop print reading, metal forming, machining, and fabrication skills through various industry-level projects produced in the program.

li.a: Welding and Fabrication Program Learning Objectives

div.p: MCC students shall be distinguishable by their critical thinking, effective communication, professionalism, and technical competency. Students will use current and contemporary practices to demonstrate mastery of the Program Learning Outcomes.

link.label: Welding, Soldering, and Brazing Machine Setters, Operators, and Tenders

system-page.name: index
system-page.title: Pre-Apprenticeship Welding Technician Certificate
system-page.summary: MCC’s Welding Technician Certificate will prepare you for an entry-level career in welding, and you can continue your studies through MCC to earn your Advanced Welding Technician Certificate. The certificate will prepare you to pass the American Welding Society (AWS) certification as an AWS Level 1—Entry Welder.
system-page.keywords: Welding Technician Certificate, welding, metal fabrication, forging, AWS Level 1—Entry Welder, American Welding Society (AWS) certification
system-page.description: Prepare for a career in pipefitting, ironworking, boiler making, and other construction-related welding fields.

banner.alt: Welding student in class wearing welding helmet

content.p: The %=ProgramTitle=% is designed for students seeking a career in pipefitting, ironworking, boiler making, and other construction-related welding fields. This certificate gives you a head start on the welding skills and foundational skills that construction-related welding apprenticeships are looking for. This certificate stacks into the Advanced Welding Technician Certificate, giving you a pathway into advanced careers in production welding. This certificate allows you to become eligible for specialized welding certifications through the American Welding Society (AWS).

a.data-acalog-program-name: Pre-Apprenticeship Welding Technician Certificate, Sample Semester Pathway

breakdown.heading: What you need to know about getting your %=ProgramTitle=% (CIP %=CIP=%)

content.h3: What makes MCC's %=ProgramTitle=% program better than, or distinct from, similar programs at other institutions?

ul.li: MCC has worked closely with local manufacturers and employers in the welding industry to ensure our courses provide the skills local employers are looking for.

There are no pre-requisite courses for the %=ProgramTitle=% introductory courses, so students in high school, adult education, or other workforce training programs can easily transition to the college to take these courses. Those with prior welding coursework may transfer it to the college for advanced placement in welding courses.
Our welding certificates complement the credit-based skills in MCC’s manufacturing and industrial maintenance programs.

content.h3: How will MCC's %=ProgramTitle=% program prepare me for my career or connect me to opportunities?

ul.li: Industry partners needing welders have reviewed and approved the curriculum, ensuring you'll learn the skills they need today. MCC meets with these industry partners annually to ensure the coursework you take is relevant to the current needs of an ever-changing industry.

Course material offered throughout the welding certificates is designed to help you develop the requisite skills to pass the certified welder tests in accordance with the American Welding Society’s D1.1 Structural Steel Code.
Courses are taught by industry experts in welding and fabrication who will discuss the many career opportunities in the welding field.

system-page.name: index
system-page.title: Automation, Robotics, and Mechatronics, AAS
system-page.summary: Earn an Associate of Applied Science in Automation, Robotics, and Mechatronics to work with advanced technology used in modern manufacturing and other areas where automation and robotics are becoming more prevalent.
system-page.keywords: programmable logic controllers, hydraulics, pneumatics
system-page.description: Work with advanced technology used in modern manufacturing.

banner.alt: A White male MCC student in his early 20's smiles as he reaches across a table to move something under a robotic arm. Another student has a tablet computer.

content.p: Prepare to meet the growing need for college-educated problem solvers who support a variety of advanced technology.

In MCC’s %=ProgramTitle=% program, you’ll gain the skills to work safely as you build, test, install, troubleshoot, and maintain robotic equipment and related automated systems. Coursework will cover the essentials of hydraulics and pneumatics, electricity, blueprint reading, motor controls, mechanical assembly, and more.

Through job shadowing, field trips, workplace tours, and career mentoring opportunities, you’ll graduate ready to work with this technology in modern manufacturing and in various areas where automation and robotics are becoming more prevalent.

Stackable MCC Certificates include:
Manufacturing Essentials Certificate > Production Technician Certificate > Industrial Maintenance Technician Certificate > Automation Technician Certificate building up to the %=ProgramTitle=%

system-page.name: index
system-page.title: Precision Machining, AAS
system-page.keywords: machinist, machining and tool inspector, computer-aided design (CAD)
system-page.description: Start your career in machining.

banner.alt: Precision industrial CNC machining of metal detail by cutting mill at factory

content.p: Precision machinists are essential in making parts for industrial equipment, medical devices, and consumer goods. These skilled professionals analyze blueprints, create designs, cut metal, build projects, and regularly maintain machinery.

In MCC’s Precision Machining program, you'll gain an in-depth understanding of the advanced computer-aided design (CAD) and computer-aided manufacturing (CAM) skills needed for programming tool location, motion, and, feeds and speeds. You’ll also receive hands-on experience using manual and CNC lathes, mills, and precision measuring equipment to design and produce a variety of parts and assemblies used in today’s ever-changing world of manufacturing.

Stackable MCC certificates in this program include:
Manufacturing Essentials Certificate > Manual Machining Certificate > CNC Operator Level I Certificate > CNC Operator Level II Certificate > CNC Programmer Certificate building up to the Precision Machining AAS

a.data-acalog-program-name: Precision Machining, AAS Sample Semester Pathway

system-page.name: index
system-page.title: Help Desk Specialist Certificate
system-page.summary: The Help Desk Certificate prepares you for an entry-level position at a company's help desk where you'll perform question/problem diagnosis and guide users through step-by-step solutions in a call center environment.
system-page.teaser: Prepare for industry-recognized certifications like CompTIA A+ and more.
system-page.keywords: Computer user support, computer network support, IT help desk
system-page.description: Troubleshoot network problems to maintain system integrity and minimize downtime.

banner.alt: a server room

content.p: The %=ProgramTitle=% prepares you for an entry-level position at a company's help desk where you'll perform question/problem diagnosis and guide users through step-by-step solutions in a call center environment. You'll learn a wide range of subjects from Microsoft Excel to networking and operating systems, carefully designed to facilitate success in several certifications, such as Microsoft Office Specialist (MOS) Excel, CompTIA IT Fundamentals+, CompTIA A+, CompTIA Network+, Microsoft MD-100, and Microsoft MD-101. Successful graduates are prepared to provide high-quality customer support and handle diverse IT issues in a help desk environment.

Stackable MCC certificates in this program include:
%=ProgramTitle=% > IT Specialist Certificate > Computer Network Support Specialist Certificate > Information Security Analyst Certificate building up to the Network Security AAS

a.data-acalog-program-name: Help Desk Specialist Certificate, Sample Semester Pathway

breakdown.heading: What you need to know about getting your %=ProgramTitle=% (CIP %=CIP=%)

content.h3: How will MCC's %=ProgramTitle=% program prepare me for my career or connect me to opportunities?
content.p: The program incorporates preparatory work for professional certification tests from CompTIA and Microsoft.

You'll be well-equipped for roles in network administration and cloud support, ready to address the intricate demands of modern digital infrastructures.

system-page.name: index
system-page.title: Cancer Registry Certificate
system-page.summary: The Cancer Registry Certificate program at MCC is intended for individuals with an associate's or bachelor's degree, especially those with a background in Health Information Management. Accredited by the National Cancer Registrars Association, the program focuses on practical training through a clinical practicum and is offered in a flexible online format.
system-page.keywords: CTR certification, RHIT certification, Cancer Tumor Registry, cancer registry certificate, tumor registrar, health information management, NCRA, Certified Tumor Registrar, cancer registry management,
system-page.description: Prepare to sit for the Cancer Tumor Registrar certification exam through the National Cancer Registrars Association.

content.p: Tumor (or cancer) registrars collect and code data that provides essential information to researchers, healthcare providers, and public health officials to advance screening programs, cancer treatment, and prevention. The %=ProgramTitle=% provides the instruction and clinical experience to develop the technical skills necessary to become a Certified Tumor Registrar and prepares you to sit for the Cancer Tumor Registrar (CTR) certification exam through the National Cancer Registrars Association (NCRA).

The %=ProgramTitle=% is intended for students who have completed an associate's or bachelor's degree.  Students with a degree in Health Information Management are at an advantage as prerequisites are already met!
The Cancer Registry Management Program at MCC is accredited by the National Cancer Registrars Association, 1330 Braddock Place, Suite 520, Alexandria, VA 22314.
Contact (815) 455-8710 or send us an email with specific questions about this program.

breakdown.heading: What you need to know about getting your %=ProgramTitle=% (CIP %=CIP=%)

content.h3: What makes MCC's %=ProgramTitle=% program better than, or distinct from, similar programs at other institutions?
content.p: Cancer Registry Management courses are taught by professionals working in the field. Classes are offered in an eight-week flexible online format and include an optional drop-in weekly video conference with faculty. The capstone course of the program is a clinical practicum that provides hands-on professional experience in cancer registry.

div.p: Christina J. Coclanis-Loding, RHIA, CCS |  (815) 455-8713
Allison Minicz, RHIA, CTR | E200 | (815) 479-7884
Students will be able to:

ol.li: Distinguish between the types of registries: hospitals and central registries.
Identify the national standard-setting organizations and professional organizations.
Explain the Standards for Commission on Cancer (CoC) Accredited Cancer Programs.
Understand the diagnosis of cancer, prevention, and treatment options.
Accurately use ICD-0, STORE Manual, AJCC Cancer Staging Manual, SEER Summary Stage General Coding Instructions, and SEER*Rx.
Accurately abstract a cancer case.
Differentiate between the types of follow-up.
Incorporate data management, data quality, and utilization into the practice of Cancer Registry Management.
Demonstrate an understanding of NCRA’s professional code of ethics.

system-page.name: index
system-page.title: Radiologic Technology, AAS
system-page.summary: The Radiologic Technology program at MCC offers an Associate of Applied Science degree designed to prepare students for careers as Radiologic Technologists in various healthcare settings. This comprehensive two-year program combines classroom instruction, laboratory work, and practical training, ensuring that students are equipped to create diagnostic images and successfully obtain national certification.
system-page.teaser: Prepare to sit for the American Registry of Radiologic Technologists (ARRT) credentialing examination.
system-page.keywords: radiography, Diagnostic Medical Imaging Radiography, radiologic technology, diagnostic radiographic images, rad tech, medical imaging
system-page.description: Become a Radiologic Technologist in two years.

image.name: radiology.jpg
image.description: Doctor looking at X-ray machine in clinic. Female doctor sets up the machine to x-ray over patient. Radiologist and patient in an x-ray room.

banner.alt: A professional in a lab coat inspects an MRI machine, representing the role of medical imaging in diagnostics.

content.p: The %=ProgramTitle=% program is designed to help you become a skilled Radiologic Technologist, ready to work in various healthcare environments, including hospitals, outpatient clinics, and doctors' offices.
Throughout the program, you'll engage in classroom learning, lab work, and practical training, enabling you to develop the skills necessary to create clear diagnostic images of the human body.

div.p: MCC's %=ProgramTitle=% program is authorized by the Illinois Community College Board and the Higher Learning Commission. MCC is currently in the process of acquiring accreditation from the Joint Review Committee on Education in Radiologic Technology (JRCERT), the accrediting body for Radiologic Technology programs. JRCERT is the only organization recognized by the United States Department of Education (USDE) and the Council for Higher Education Accreditation (CHEA) for accrediting traditional and distance educational programs in radiography, radiation therapy, magnetic resonance imaging, and medical dosimetry. JRCERT is the “gold standard” of accreditation for educational programs in the radiologic sciences.

All inquiries about the program's accreditation status should be directed by mail to the Joint Review Committee on Education in Radiologic Technology, Chief Executive Officer, 20 North Wacker Drive, Suite 2850, Chicago, IL 60606-2901; by phone at (312) 704-5300; or by email at mail@jrcert.org.

The %=ProgramTitle=% Program at McHenry County College began its inaugural cohort in Fall 2025. As this is the first cohort, no program outcome data are currently available for graduation rates, retention rates, or the American Registry of Radiologic Technologists (ARRT) pass rates. These outcome measures will be collected and published in accordance with JRCERT requirements. They will be made publicly available on the McHenry County College %=ProgramTitle=% Program website following the completion of the first cohort’s educational program in May 2027.

breakdown.heading: What you need to know about getting your %=ProgramTitle=% (CIP %=CIP=%)

content.h3: What makes MCC's %=ProgramTitle=% program better than or distinct from similar programs at other institutions?
content.p: This two-year program offers a cost-effective way to train as a Radiographer, providing the same high-quality education and hands-on experience typically found at a four-year university.
content.h3: How will MCC's %=ProgramTitle=% program prepare me for my career or connect me to opportunities?
content.p: By the time you graduate, you'll be prepared to take the national certification exam from the American Registry of Radiologic Technologists (ARRT), which opens the door to entry-level jobs in diagnostic imaging across various healthcare settings. MCC’s %=ProgramTitle=% program focuses on both classroom instruction and practical application, setting you up for success in this critical field.
content.h3: Outside of the Classroom
content.p: Over five semesters, you'll get valuable real-world experience in some of the best hospitals in the Chicago area, applying what you've learned in a professional setting.

div.p: Rhonda Knaack, M.S.R.S., MHA, R.T. (R)(CT), ARRT, ARDMS, AB, OB/GYN, RVT(VT) | E212F | (815) 444-8938
Adriana Salazar, BS, R.T. (R)(CT), ARRT | (815) 479-7889

The mission of the Associate in Applied Science Radiologic Technology Program at McHenry County College (MCC) is to educate and graduate at an entry-level competent, high-quality, and professional Radiologic Technologists for the healthcare workforce and for graduates to provide excellent care for the diverse healthcare population.
Students will:

ol.li:
Graduates of the %=ProgramTitle=% program as entry-level Radiographers should be competent in the following skills:
Demonstrate radiation safety practices for both them and their patients.
Demonstrate knowledge in the critique of radiographic images.
Demonstrate accuracy in all radiographic positions

Graduates of the %=ProgramTitle=% program will demonstrate knowledge and application of critical thinking skills:
Demonstrates the ability to modify radiographic images and procedures to meet the patient needs.
Demonstrates the ability to alter technical factors and positioning for radiographic images to accommodate the patient’s pathologic conditions.

Graduates of the %=ProgramTitle=% program will demonstrate proper interpersonal skills:
Demonstrate proper communication with the patient.
Demonstrates proper accuracy in documentation of patient histories.
Demonstrates the ability to communicate with a diverse patient population.

Graduates of the %=ProgramTitle=% program will demonstrate healthcare professionalism:
Demonstrates professional behavior with patients and other healthcare professionals.
Demonstrates and comply with all healthcare professional ethics.
Demonstrates and verbalizes the importance of continued professional development.

div.p: The %=ProgramTitle=% Program will effectively meet the needs of the students and the community it serves by:

ul.li: Students of the %=ProgramTitle=% program will successfully pass the national ARRT board exam, and acquire the designation Radiologic Technologist Registered, R.T. (R).
Students will complete all aspects of the %=ProgramTitle=% program.
Students will gain employment in the Radiologic Technology field.
Students will be satisfied with their education in the %=ProgramTitle=% program.
Employers will demonstrate a significant level of satisfaction with the graduates of the %=ProgramTitle=% program.

div.p: The %=ProgramTitle=% program's mission is confirmed through successful completion and achieving the goals set forth by this program and as outlined in this Student Handbook. MCC’s %=ProgramTitle=% program’s mission, goals, and outcomes are evaluated annually by members of the McHenry County College %=ProgramTitle=% Program Advisory Committee. The committee’s participation in the evaluation process of the %=ProgramTitle=% program is essential in serving the community MCC serves.

ul.li: Updated Fieldwork Performance Evaluation Tool

system-page.name: index
system-page.title: Administrative Office Technologies, AAS
system-page.summary: The Administrative Office Technologies AAS provides the career training you need to become an administrative assistant, office manager, administrative manager, and many other office professional roles.
system-page.keywords: office management, office skills, computer skills, administrative office technology, secretary, secretarial skills, Administrative Office Management, AOM
system-page.description: Learn the essential skills that employers value in administrative professionals.

banner.alt: Administrative Office manager analyzing data

content.p: MCC’s %=ProgramTitle=% program provides the career training you need to become an administrative assistant, office manager, administrative manager, and many other office professional roles. The curriculum focuses on the latest application software and technologies used, virtual technologies, office procedures, keyboarding, and communication skills. Upon completion of the program, you will have the technological, administrative, and interpersonal skills—as well as the initiative, confidence, and decision-making ability—to be an effective member of an office team.

Upon completion of advanced-level software courses, you are eligible to sit for one or more of the Microsoft Office Specialist™ (MOS) exams. Having these certifications can open doors to advancement and new workplace opportunities.

MCC’s program allows you to build on your skills with stackable certificates in Word Processing, Spreadsheets, Microsoft Skills, Virtual Office Assistant, Medical Administration, Desktop Publishing, or Administrative Office Technologies that you can earn as you work toward your AAS degree.

If you believe you are proficient in the knowledge and skills taught in a particular AOT course, the AOT Department offers several proficiency exams, similar to a final exam, that may be completed to demonstrate your knowledge and skill to receive course credit. Read about the proficiency exam procedures »

breakdown.heading: What you need to know about getting your %=ProgramTitle=% (CIP %=CIP=%)

content.h3: What makes MCC's %=ProgramTitle=% better than or distinct from similar programs at other institutions?

ul.li: Credits earned in the certificate programs are stackable towards the AOT AAS degree (see above), helping you achieve your educational goals faster, and earning multiple credentials as you go.
Students completing this degree will complete the Word, Excel, PowerPoint, and Outlook Microsoft Office Specialist (MOS) certification exams, earning nationally recognized credentials.
Many AOT courses have reduced or zero textbook costs, with more courses added each academic year.
Classes include course projects common to industry.
You may participate in Scots for Non-Profits, volunteering at a local non-profit organization, toward earning graduation Silver Cord hours.
You'll learn from educated and experienced faculty who will share their valuable experience, knowledge, and troubleshooting skills with you.

content.h3: How will MCC's %=ProgramTitle=% program prepare me for my career or connect me to opportunities?
content.p: Ask about internships for college credit and job shadowing opportunities!
content.h3: What have graduates gone on to do?
content.p: Students retooled or refined their skills towards a promotion or advancing or changing careers. Some students have secured a part-time or “stepping-stone” position after certificate completion while working towards the degree. Other students have completed the degree to obtain their Microsoft Office Specialist (MOS) Certifications to improve or enhance their skills and knowledge towards excelling in their careers.
content.h3: Outside the Classroom
content.p: To further your career, you may seek national certification credentials beyond MCC's certificate. Organizations offering certification exams include:

div.p: The mission of McHenry County College’s Administrative Office Technologies program is to develop students who are skilled in current software, technology, keyboarding, document formatting, communication, and interpersonal skills. MCC students shall be distinguishable by their critical thinking, effective communication, professionalism, and technical competency. Students will use current and contemporary practices to demonstrate mastery of the Program Learning Outcomes.
Students will:

ol.li: Demonstrate proficiency and use of current software relevant to the administrative field.
Demonstrate accurate and efficient keyboarding skills.
Apply problem-solving and technology skills in a business environment.
Proofread, review, and edit to create error-free documents.
Use discipline-specific terminology in verbal and written communications.

banner.alt: woman in wheel chair working in office

content.p: Take your Word Processing skills to the next level with MCC’s %=ProgramTitle=%. You'll learn skills like formatting text, paragraphs, and pages, document collaboration, track changes, forms, charts, tables, graphic elements, mail merge, and more!

As part of this certificate program, you will sit for the Microsoft Office Specialist™ (MOS) Word certification exam upon completion of the level II Word Processing course. This industry-recognized credential shows an employer you have the knowledge and skills desired.

The %=ProgramTitle=% is stackable, meaning credits earned may be applied towards the Microsoft Skills certificate, Virtual Office Assistant certificate, Administrative Office Technologies certificate, and Administrative Office Technologies AAS.

AREA.value: Marketing, Finance, Business Management and Administration

a.data-acalog-program-name: Word Processing Certificate, Sample Semester Pathway

breakdown.heading: What you need to know about getting your %=ProgramTitle=% (CIP %=CIP=%)

content.h3: What makes MCC's %=ProgramTitle=% program better than or distinct from similar programs at other institutions?

ul.li: Credits earned in this certificate are stackable towards other AOT certificates and the AOT AAS degree (see above), helping you achieve your educational goals faster, earning multiple credentials as you go.
Students completing this certificate will complete the Word MOS certification exam, a nationally recognized credential.
Many AOT courses have reduced or zero textbook costs, with more courses added each academic year.
Classes include course projects common to industry.
You may participate in Scots for Non-Profits, volunteering at a local non-profit organization, toward earning graduation Silver Cord hours.
You'll learn from educated and experienced faculty who will share their valuable experience, knowledge, and troubleshooting skills with you.

content.h3: How will MCC's %=ProgramTitle=% program prepare me for my career or connect me to opportunities?
content.p: Ask about internships for college credit and job shadowing opportunities!
content.h3: Outside of the Classroom
content.p: To further your career, you may seek national certification credentials beyond MCC's certificate. Organizations offering certification exams include:

link.label: Executive Secretaries and Executive Administrative Assistants
Secretaries and Administrative Assistants, except Legal, Medical, and Executive

system-page.name: index
system-page.title: Architectural Technology, AAS
system-page.summary: Study the ideas, methods, and processes behind how architecture is conceptualized, designed, and built.
system-page.keywords: Computer Aided Design, CAD, drafting
system-page.description: Study how buildings are constructed. Focus on the latest software and design.

banner.alt: Architectural Engineering Technology

content.p: In the %=ProgramTitle=% program, you'll study the ideas, methods, and processes behind how architecture is conceptualized, designed, and built. The curriculum focuses on the latest building delivery processes, software, design processes, and communication methods.

Graduates of the program can seek employment in architecture and building design-related firms as entry-level designers or in architecture, engineering, and construction (AEC) firms as entry-level computer-aided design (CAD) and building information modeling (BIM) technicians or transfer their credits to a bachelor's degree program to continue their education.
Choose the option that best suits your educational and professional goals.

div.p: The %=ProgramTitle=% (Design Option) focuses on the principles of architectural design and the methods used to communicate them to clients and stakeholders. If you pursue the Design Option, you can also transfer to an architecture program offering a bachelor's degree, allowing you to pursue an architectural license.

a.data-acalog-link-text: Architectural Technology, AAS (Design Option) Sample Semester Pathway
a.data-acalog-program-name: Architectural Technology, AAS (Design Option) Sample Semester Pathway

div.p: Our program is designed to transfer to four-year universities offering a Bachelor of Arts or Science in architectural studies (pre-professional degree) or a Bachelor of Architecture (professional degree), which allows students to pursue architectural licensing. MCC is working on several articulation agreements to help students transfer directly to select regional architecture schools and will enable them to pursue various paths to complete the academic requirements to become licensed architects.

a.data-acalog-link-text: Architectural Technology, AAS Sample Transfer Pathway
a.data-acalog-program-name: Architectural Technology, AAS Sample Transfer Pathway

a.data-acalog-link-text: Architectural Technology, AAS Transfer Degree Requirements
a.data-acalog-program-name: Architectural Technology, AAS Sample Transfer Pathway

a.data-acalog-link-text: Architectural Technology, AAS Transfer Degree Planner
a.data-acalog-program-name: Architectural Technology, AAS Sample Transfer Pathway

div.p: The %=ProgramTitle=% (Technology Option) focuses on the specific software and workflows used in the building delivery process, specifically computer-aided design (CAD) and building information modeling (BIM).

a.data-acalog-link-text: Architectural Technology, AAS (Technology Option) Sample Semester Pathway
a.data-acalog-program-name: Architectural Technology, AAS (Technology Option) Sample Semester Pathway

section.heading: What makes MCC's Architectural and Engineering Design Technology Certificate program better than or distinct from similar programs at other institutions?

content.p: MCC offers your choice of three specialization options:

ol.li: Engineering Technology Specialization Option
Architectural Technology Specialization Option
Interior Design Specialization Option

content.p: The AET certificate is offered in conjunction with the Fast Track. The Fast Track is a cohort-based program. Through the Fast Track, you can earn a certificate in two years or less.

MCC has experienced instructors who have proven themselves in the field and a newly renovated design center that features Angle-CAD design workstations, AutoCAD software, AutoDesk Inventor 3D software and new computers.

PAGE.title: Architectural and Engineering Design Technology Certificate
PAGE.summary: The Architectural and Engineering Design certificate serves students seeking to enter the drafting and design field as well as those looking to develop their skills and advance in their careers.
PAGE.keywords: Architectural and Engineering Design Technology Certificate, Computer Aided Design, CAD, drafting
PAGE.description: Prepare to enter the drafting and design field or develop your skills and advance in your career.

PAGE.name: index
PAGE.title: Architectural Technology Certificate
PAGE.summary: Gain in-demand skills with our Architectural Technology Certificate. Master CAD, BIM, and latest project delivery methods. Prepare for an entry-level career in AEC. This certificate can also lead to the AAS in Architectural Technology.
PAGE.keywords: Architectural and Engineering Design Technology Certificate, Computer Aided Design, CAD, drafting
PAGE.description: Master CAD, BIM, and latest project delivery methods.

PAGE.name: index
PAGE.title: Architectural Design Certificate
PAGE.summary: Study the ideas, methods, and processes behind how architecture is conceptualized, designed, and built.
PAGE.keywords: Architectural Design Certificate, Architectural Design, Computer Aided Design, CAD, drafting
PAGE.description: Study how buildings are constructed. Focus on the latest software and design.

breakdown.heading: What you need to know about getting your %=ProgramTitle=% (CIP %=CIP=%)

content.h3: What makes MCC's %=ProgramTitle=% program better than, or distinct from, similar programs at other institutions?
content.p: MCC's curriculum incorporates the latest digital and analog architectural creation and presentation methods taught within a newly created, dedicated design studio within the College's recently completed Foglia Center for Advanced Technology (CATI). Industry-focused design software includes the latest versions of Autodesk AutoCAD, Autodesk Revit, Rhino 3D, and the Adobe Creative Suite. Access to fabrication tools includes laser cutting, CNC machining, and 3D printing.
content.h3: How will MCC's %=ProgramTitle=% program prepare me for my career or connect me to opportunities?
content.p: Stackable certificates are available for each option, allowing you to enter the workforce quickly while building toward an AAS degree.
content.h3: Outside of the Classroom

li.a: National Council of Architectural Registration Boards

content.p: The %=ProgramTitle=% provides you with a foundation in architectural technology focused on the latest methods of digital project delivery.  The curriculum focuses on industry-recognized architectural software applications, including computer-aided design (CAD) and building information modeling (BIM), and project delivery processes, preparing you for a career as an entry-level CAD or BIM technician with architectural, engineering, and construction (AEC) firms.  This certificate can also be a first step towards completing the Associate in Applied Science in Architectural Technology – Technology Option degree.

The curriculum, grounded in real-world applications and cutting-edge technology, prepares you for entry-level work related to video production and editing, with an emphasis on equipment and set basics.

content.h3: What makes MCC's %=ProgramTitle=% program better than or distinct from similar programs at other institutions?
content.p: MCC's %=ProgramTitle=% program is taught by dedicated instructors who are working in the field of Video Production and Editing. Opportunities to use video, lighting, and sound recording equipment, Macintosh computer labs, and access to education in related video production fields allow you to enter the workforce prepared for professional design careers and ready to transfer to four-year institutions.
content.h3: How will MCC's %=ProgramTitle=% program prepare me for my career or connect me to opportunities?
content.p: MCC's %=ProgramTitle=% prepares you not just in design skills but in professional skills, workflow, and design habits that will give certificate earners an advantage in video production. You'll develop writing, math, and critical thinking skills that apply to numerous professional situations.
content.h3: Outside of the Classroom
content.p: Students in the %=ProgramTitle=% program can collaborate with other students, screen work, and participate in VOICES magazine.

banner.alt: A young male Black student sits in front of a set of computer monitors editing video files.

content.p: In the %=ProgramTitle=% program, you'll earn an Associate of Applied Science degree that equips you with the knowledge, skills, and abilities needed for entry-level Audio/Video technology positions. You'll learn all aspects of this field, including equipment setup and maintenance, production practices, and video and audio editing and post-production. This program will guide you through industry-standard workflows from pre-production to post-production. You'll also develop specialized skills for various careers through courses in Special Effects and Animation, Graphic Design, Marketing, and Fine Arts. This comprehensive education will prepare you for a successful career in this dynamic and evolving field.

system-page.name: index
system-page.title: Commercial Heating II Certificate
system-page.summary: The Commercial Heating II Certificate program trains students as entry-level HVAC technicians, with a focus on commercial systems, including gas and electric heating, boilers, and rooftop units. It includes hands-on servicing, troubleshooting, diagnostics, and offers an EPA Section 608 Certification. The program features internships and learning at the MCC Foglia Center for Advanced Technology.
system-page.keywords: commercial, Heating, Ventilation, and Air Conditioning, Commercial Heating II
system-page.description: Train for commercial heating systems.

banner.alt: A technician works on a roof-top chiller.

content.p: The %=ProgramTitle=% prepares entry-level heating, ventilation, and air conditioning (HVAC) technicians to work on various commercial heating systems. You'll develop a basic understanding of commercial gas and electrical heating systems, boilers, rooftop heating systems, direct digital controls, and the properties of air. Training includes electrical troubleshooting, servicing and cleaning commercial heating systems, and multimeter and diagnostic test equipment use.

system-page.name: index
system-page.title: Web Development Certificate
system-page.summary: The Web Development Certificate is an all-encompassing program that merges both back-end and front-end development skills.
system-page.keywords: Web Developer, UI/UX Designer, Web Designer
system-page.description: Acquire a comprehensive skill set to launch a career in full-stack web development.

banner.alt: two male app developers collaborating in front of a computer

content.p: The %=ProgramTitle=% is an all-encompassing program that includes both back-end and front-end development skills. On the back end, you'll explore server-side processes and database design and get hands-on experience with Amazon Web Services (AWS) cloud solutions. The front-end curriculum focuses on HTML, CSS, and JavaScript, with an emphasis on responsive design, accessibility standards, and User Experience Design/User Interface Design (UX/UI) principles.
Once you complete the %=ProgramTitle=%, you'll be fully equipped for roles in server/database management and front-end web development for web and mobile applications.

system-page.name: index
system-page.title: Nursing, AAS
system-page.summary: The Nursing AAS program is designed to prepare you to function as a professional nurse in a variety of healthcare settings including hospitals, nursing homes, clinics, and private homes and offices.
system-page.teaser: Enroll in MCC's accredited Associate's Degree in Nursing program, recognized for excellence with a 100% NCLEX-RN pass rate. Start your nursing career today!
system-page.keywords: Nursing, ADN, nursing, registered nurses, registered nurse, nursing program, nursing information session schedule, healthcare, health, Health Career Programs
system-page.description: Prepare to function as a professional nurse in a variety of healthcare settings.

banner.alt: closeup of MCC nursing patch

content.p: The Registered Nursing AAS program is designed to prepare you to function as a professional nurse in various healthcare settings, including hospitals, nursing homes, clinics, and private homes and offices. Become an RN at a fraction of the cost of a four-year university.

div.p:
MCC's Associate Degree in Nursing program has been recognized as a
"Top Performing Nursing School in Illinois" for 2026 by RegisteredNursing.org, highlighting our students' achievements of a 100% NCLEX-RN pass rate in 2023 and 2024.

content.p: The MCC nursing program is approved by the Illinois Department of Financial and Professional Regulation, the Illinois Community College Board, and the Illinois Board of Higher Education. Graduates of the program are eligible to take the National Council of State Boards of Nursing Examination (NCLEX-RN®), which may lead to licensure as a Registered Professional Nurse (RN).

p.strong: The Associate Degree in Nursing program at McHenry County College, located in Crystal Lake, IL, is accredited by the:

div.p:
Accreditation Commission for Education in Nursing (ACEN)
3390 Peachtree Road NE, Suite 1400
Atlanta, GA 30326 404-975-5000

img.alt: Accreditation Commission for Education in Nursing (ACEN) logo

content.p: The latest accreditation decision by the ACEN Board of Commissioners for the associate nursing program is to grant Continuing Accreditation and schedule the next review for Fall 2032.

header.h3: Program Outcomes of the Nursing Program

table.caption: Annual pass rates of first-time National Council Licensure Examination-RN (NCLEX-RN) takers
Percent of nursing students that complete the program
Percent of students that are employed in the field of nursing within one year of graduation

section.p: The Associate in Applied Science (AAS) Nursing Program at McHenry County College meets the state education requirements for a Registered Nurse license in the State of Illinois. McHenry County College has not determined if the AAS Nursing Program at McHenry County College meets the state education requirements in any other state, any U.S. Territory, or the District of Columbia. Contact the state regulatory agency for nursing in any other state for which this information is needed.
The National Council of State Boards of Nursing (NCSBN) has resources that may be helpful.

content.h3: Requirements and Guidelines

li.a: Frequently Asked Questions about the Nursing AAS
End of Nursing Program Student Learning Outcomes

content.p: Contact (815) 455-8710 or send us an email with specific questions about this program.

breakdown.heading: What you need to know about getting your %=ProgramTitle=% (CIP %=CIP=%)

content.h3: A Word About Costs
content.p: To become a licensed registered nurse, you must successfully pass the National Council Licensure Examination for Registered Nurses (NCLEX-RN). See the National Council of State Boards of Nursing, Inc. (NCSBN) website for current cost information regarding registration and licensure fees.

Be aware that as a student in the nursing program, you will also incur costs including, but not limited to books, vaccinations, background checks, medical exams, Tuberculosis (TB) testing, drug screening, health insurance, and CPR (Basic Life Support) certification.
Since the 2020-2021 catalog, total degree credit hours equal 66-70. Total degree hours will be 70 for students needing BIO 157.
content.h3: What makes MCC's Nursing AAS program better than, or distinct from, similar programs at other institutions?

ul.li: MCC nursing program graduates pass the NCLEX at a rate that is above both state and national averages for both associate and bachelor degree programs.
MCC partners with four-year institutions to allow you an easy transition into a BSN program after graduating from MCC. See RN to BSN transfer guides.

content.h3: How will MCC's Nursing AAS program prepare me for my career or connect me to opportunities?

ul.li: Graduates of the program are eligible to take the National Council of State Boards of Nursing Examination (NCLEX-RN), which may lead to licensure as a Registered Professional Nurse (RN).
You will experience 12 hours of hands-on, clinical instruction per week in a hospital or healthcare setting, beginning with the first semester and continuing throughout the program.
Graduates are well-versed in and prepared to use the technological tools and systems that are the standard in healthcare today.

content.h3: What have graduates gone on to do?

ul.li: Students have obtained employment at Advocate Health Care, Northwestern, Presence Health, Mercy Health System, and several area assisted-living and long-term care facilities.
Our graduates have worked in nearly every facet of nursing, from ER, OB, ICU, acute care, hospice, physician offices, assisted living, telehealth, and long-term care.
Some graduates have received bachelor's degrees in nursing and are currently enrolled and pursuing their master's degrees.

content.h3: Outside the Classroom

ul.li: MCC's Student Nursing Organization (SNO)
National Organization for Associate Degree Nursing (N-OADN)
National Student Nurses Association (NSNA)

div.p: The philosophy of the Associate Degree Nursing (ADN) program is congruent with the McHenry County College mission statement and is supported by the works of Marjory Gordon and the National League for Nursing. The faculty believes that:

Each individual is a unique, holistic being with bio-psychosocial, cultural, and spiritual dimensions in constant interaction with the environment. All human beings have in common certain functional patterns that contribute to their health, quality of life, and achievement of human potential.

Health is an optimal level of functioning that allows individuals, families, or communities to develop their potential to the fullest. Health is measured by parameters and norms, combined with a client’s perception, and includes multi-dimensional states of health and illness. Ideally, health is consistent with individual potential and allows nursing intervention to be individualized.

The environment is an aggregate of all the conditions and socio-cultural influences affecting the life and development of a person. Interaction between the client and the environment is an essential, common thread running through all functional patterns. The environment impacts the individual’s functional and dysfunctional patterns.

Nursing is the art and science of applying a specialized body of knowledge and skills in providing evidence-based, clinically competent care. The nursing process is used as a basis for nursing care decisions and client interaction. The goals of nursing include promoting independence, maintaining and restoring health, or supporting a peaceful death.

Nursing education belongs within institutions of higher learning and supports educational mobility. The affordability and accessibility of the community college provide an ideal setting for associate degree nursing education. The curriculum combines study in nursing and related disciplines. Associate degree nursing education is responsive to current economic, social, demographic, and political forces, and to technological changes in transforming healthcare delivery.

Learning is an individual and lifelong process evidenced by changed behavior resulting from the acquisition of knowledge, skills, understanding, and attitudes. Learning in an educational setting is enhanced by a teacher/student relationship in which the teacher’s responsibility is to structure and facilitate optimal conditions for critical thinking and learning through clearly defined educational competencies. The student brings to this relationship the willingness to learn and is accountable for his/her education. Recognizing that both the rate and style of learning differ with individuals, various strategies are utilized to facilitate the student’s achievement of program competencies, attainment of maximum potential, and promotion of continued learning.

The ADN graduate, having achieved the program competencies, is prepared to practice in a variety of settings within the parameters of individual knowledge and experience according to the standards of practice. The role of the ADN graduate included provider of care, manager of care and member of the discipline. Encompassed within these roles are the core components of professional behaviors, communication, nursing process, clinical decision-making, caring interventions, teaching and learning, collaboration, and managing care.
References:
Council of ADN Competencies Task Force, National League for Nursing (2000). Competencies for graduates of ADN programs. Sudbury, MA: Jones & Bartlett Publisher.
Gordon, M. (1986). Nursing diagnosis: Process and application. 3rd edition, St. Louis: Mosby.

The mission of McHenry County College’s Nursing Degree Program is to develop students who are skilled in the profession of nursing, which is recognized as the environment, patient, health, and nurse. MCC students shall be distinguishable by their critical thinking, effective communication, professionalism, and technical competency. Students will use current and contemporary practices to demonstrate mastery of the Program Learning Outcomes.

ol.li: Demonstrate the attitudes, values, and behaviors consistent with the standards of professional nursing practice.
Demonstrate effective therapeutic communication with clients, families, significant others, and members of the healthcare team.
Utilize the nursing process as a basis for clinical decision-making in providing client, family, and community care.
Incorporate evidence-based interventions in individualized plans of care.
Provide individualized nursing care to diverse patients across the lifespan.
Adhere to professional standards to maintain the safety of self, patient, and environment.

system-page.name: index
system-page.title: Entrepreneurial Agriculture, AAS
system-page.summary: In the Entrepreneurial Agriculture program, you'll receive foundational business and horticulture training, and gain hands-on experience growing food crops.
system-page.keywords: Entrepreneurial Agriculture, farms, farm laborers, farm managers, farming, greenhouse managers, school garden managers, horticultural business, agricultural business
system-page.description: Combine business and horticulture training with hands-on experience growing food crops.

banner.alt: agricultural student working in garden

content.p: Growing food and starting a business both require creativity, specific skills, and an understanding of how interrelated systems work. In the Entrepreneurial Agriculture program, you'll receive foundational business and horticulture training – as well as gain hands-on experience growing food crops. You'll better understand the way food is grown and distributed so that you can make the supply chain work to your advantage.
After completing this degree, you'll be trained in the fundamentals of food production and business, including:

ul.li: Analyzing and designing food production systems for growing vegetable, fruit, and nut species for the Upper Midwest
Propagating, growing, and maintaining plants in agricultural food production systems
Applying ecologically sustainable principles in agriculture while maintaining productivity and economic viability
Working with hydroponic and aquaponics systems
Creating and implementing a business plan, starting a farm business, and scaling operations
Foundational concepts in marketing, finance, and sales, including the use of effective communication strategies with suppliers and customers

content.p: This degree program is offered in collaboration with the Center for Agrarian Learning.

system-page.summary: MCC’s Administrative Office Management program provides the career training you need to become an office manager, administrative assistant, legal or medical office assistant, receptionist, office clerk, or office support specialist
system-page.keywords: office management, office skills, computer skills, administrative office technology, secretary, secretarial skills, Administrative Office Management, AOM
system-page.description: Learn the essential skills that employers value in administrative professionals.

banner.alt: Administrative Office manager analyzing data

content.p: MCC’s Administrative Office Management program provides the career training you need to become an office manager, administrative assistant, administrative manager, coordinator, office support specialist, and many other administrative roles. The curriculum focuses on human relations and communication skills while teaching the latest technology and procedures.

Upon completion of the program, you'll have the technological, administrative, and interpersonal skills—as well as the initiative, confidence, and decision-making ability—to be an effective member of an office team.

Not sure if you’re ready to pursue the entire AOM degree? Start with just one stackable certificate in Administrative Office Management or Administrative Office Skills. Take the next step by completing both certificates. Pretty soon, the credits you earn in those certificates will add up—and you’ll be well on your way to earning your full degree!

This industry credential shows employers you have the skills needed to use the software effectively and efficiently. Having these certifications can open doors to advancement and new work opportunities.

content.h3: Outside the Classroom
content.p: In order to further your career, you may wish to seek national certification credentials beyond MCC's certificate. Organizations offering certification exams include:

div.p: The mission of McHenry County College’s Administrative Office Management Program is to develop students who are skilled in current software, technology, keyboarding, document formatting, communication, and interpersonal skills. MCC students shall be distinguishable by their critical thinking, effective communication, professionalism, and technical competency. Students will use current and contemporary practices to demonstrate mastery in the Program Learning Outcomes.
Students will:

ol.li: Demonstrate proficiency and use of current software relevant to the administrative field.
Key at advanced levels to meet employer needs and industry standards, using correct technique, without visual assistance.
Apply problem-solving skills to key and format business documents to solve real-life situations, using correct formatting guidelines.
Proofread, review, and edit in creating error-free documents.
Appropriately use discipline-specific terminology in verbal and written communications.
Choose appropriate technology for use effectively and efficiently.

div.p: Julie Freelove, Faculty and Department Chair

system-page.title: Baking and Pastry Management, AAS
system-page.summary: An AAS in Baking and Pastry Management is a great foundation for employment or starting your own business. You may also elect to continue studying toward a bachelor’s degree.
system-page.teaser: Culinary courses cover specialty breads, desserts, pastries, and confections in extensive hands-on classes—starting the very first week. You’ll also learn basic nutrition, menu development, communication, food safety, and cost control.
system-page.keywords: Associate in Applied Science degree in Baking and Pastry, Baking and Pastry AAS, culinary, chef, pastry, baking
system-page.description: Learn through lectures, demonstrations, and hands-on applications in our commercial-grade kitchen labs.

banner.alt: baker helping customer with pastries

content.p: Combine your creativity with an appreciation for beauty and precision in MCC’s %=ProgramTitle=% program. Whether your love is bread, pastries, cake designing, or a little bit of everything, our students also learn that it’s passion that’s often the secret ingredient.

As a culinary student, you’ll learn through lectures, demonstrations, and hands-on applications in our commercial-grade kitchen labs. Your courses cover specialty breads, desserts, pastries, and confections in extensive hands-on classes—starting the very first week. You’ll also learn basic nutrition, menu development, communication, food safety, and cost control. The campus and community will experience your creations in MCC’s Sweet Scot Bakery; Tartan Bistro, MCC’s student-run restaurant; and at special events hosted by the College.
A %=ProgramTitle=% is a great foundation for employment or starting your own business. You may also elect to continue study toward a bachelor’s degree.

Stackable MCC certificates in this program include: Baking and Pastry I Certificate > Baking and Pastry II Certificate > Beverage Management Certificate > Assistant Restaurant Manager Certificate building up to the Baking and Pastry Management AAS

All students in lab skills classes must purchase their own approved MCC student uniform, hat, and tool kit from the MCC Store by the second week of classes. We suggest you pre-order your supplies from the MCC Store online and pick them up from the store, B126.

a.data-acalog-program-name: Baking and Pastry Management, AAS Sample Semester Pathway

breakdown.heading: What you need to know about getting your %=ProgramTitle=% (CIP %=CIP=%)

section.h3: How do I know this program is right for me?

ul.li: Are you able to maintain a professional-level attendance record?
Are you able to perform repetitive tasks for an extended amount of time without assistance?
Are you physically and mentally able to work in situations with time limits?
Are you able to lift up to 50-pound boxes or bags of product?
Are you able to handle and grip a knife safely and efficiently without assistance?
Are you able to read and write clearly and concentrate effectively without assistance?
Are you able to perform basic math skills without the aid of a calculator?

section.h3: What makes MCC's %=ProgramTitle=% program better than or distinct from similar programs at other institutions?
section.p: All culinary and pastry courses are taught by professionals working in the field, including sous chefs and executive chefs as well as restaurant and bakery owners. These professionals make career information available to you throughout the program. In addition, toward the end of the program, the career services coordinator will meet with classes to help you construct a resume, begin the job search process, participate in mock interviews, and other activities that will prepare you for your career search and success in the baking and pastry field.
Be sure to apply for MCC scholarships to help defray the cost of your education. We have several culinary scholarships.
section.h3: How will MCC's %=ProgramTitle=% program prepare me for my career or connect me to opportunities?
section.p: The curriculum is designed to meet industry standards set by the American Culinary Federation (ACF) as well as the National Restaurant Association Educational Foundation (NRAEF).
The NRAEF prepares students for the NRAEF ManageFirst® industry certification as well as the ServSafe® Sanitation Manager certification.
section.h3: What have graduates gone on to do?
section.p: Graduates have gone on to other institutions such as Kendall College, Roosevelt University, Northern Illinois University, and Southern Illinois University to further their culinary arts or pastry education. See information on transferring a two-year AAS degree to a four-year Culinary/Hospitality/Pastry program.

In addition to standard coursework, you have the opportunity to enroll in a working classroom via Tartan Bistro, our student-run restaurant, and Sweet Scots Bakery, our student-run bakery. With department chair permission, you may also apply to be paid interns in either the student-run bakery or restaurant.

system-page.name: index
system-page.title: Construction Management, AAS
system-page.keywords: Construction Management, construction management, construction, construction certificate, construction applications management, construction application management
system-page.description: Complete selected Construction Management courses to prepare to sit for the International Code Council (ICC) Examinations.

banner.alt: construction workers looking over blueprints

section.p: You'll find that the %=ProgramTitle=% equips you with the skills to delve into the technology and management of construction projects. The curriculum immerses you in the latest construction technology, means and methods, and project delivery software while grounding you in the management principles and processes essential for successful project execution.

Upon graduation, you can pursue employment in general and specialty construction contracting and management firms, starting as an entry-level assistant project manager or project engineer. Alternatively, you can transfer your credits to a bachelor’s degree program to further your education.
section.h3: Hear what Construction Management AAS Students have to say.
section.blockquote:
"When I enrolled into the construction management program I did not know exactly what to expect and or what my goals were. After the first few classes I started to realize how I was actually enjoying school for the first time in a long time. That made me want to continue on and set a goal to finish the program."
Kyle Berge, Construction Management, AAS, May 2017

system-page.name: index
system-page.title: Criminal Justice, AAS
system-page.summary: MCC’s Criminal Justice AAS helps you develop the skills, knowledge, and attitude necessary for employment and advancement as a law enforcement officer. The degree also serves as a foundation for associated professions that require advanced studies, such as probation, parole, corrections, and social services.
system-page.keywords: Criminal Justice, Criminal Justice AAS, Harriet Johnson, William Brogan
system-page.description: Develop the skills, knowledge, and attitude necessary for employment and advancement as a law enforcement officer.

banner.alt: criminal justice in wood letters

content.p: MCC’s Criminal Justice degree develops students who are skilled in the basic concepts and terminology needed to analyze the process of American criminal justice as it unfolds daily across the country.

Classes introduce ethical critical thinking skills, training for various public policy situations, the administrative side of the criminal justice system, and substantive and procedural criminal law. We’ll also explore topics such as the judicial process, corrections, criminology, and community policing.

The program provides the foundation for you to begin an exciting and rewarding career in criminal justice or to graduate ready to continue your studies. The degree is also a good fit if you're working in the criminal justice field and looking to advance in your career.

PATHWAY.TITLE: Criminal Justice, Associate in Applied Science (AAS) Degree

content.h3: What makes MCC's %=ProgramTitle=% better than or distinct from similar programs at other institutions?

ul.li: All classes are taught by local, real-world practitioners in the field. Our academic classes are instructed by master's degree-educated professionals who are either retired or still working.
We educate students on the academic discipline while incorporating real field experience.
Our classes go further and incorporate guest speakers and trips that feature actual McHenry County area subject matter experts and facilities.

link.label: Criminal Justice and Law Enforcement Teachers, Postsecondary

div.p: The mission of McHenry County College’s Criminal Justice Program is to develop students who are skilled in the basic concepts and terminology they will need to intelligently analyze the process of American Criminal Justice as it unfolds on a daily basis across the country. MCC students shall be distinguishable by their critical thinking, effective communication, professionalism, and technical competency. Students will use current and contemporary practices to demonstrate mastery in the Program Learning Outcomes. Students who graduate from the criminal justice program may work in law enforcement as a police officer, school resource officer, evidence technician, a community service officer, or may work in the court system or correctional system. Though the AAS is not designed as a transfer degree, students may also choose to pursue a bachelor’s degree after completing the AAS in Criminal Justice.

ol.li: Students will be able to analyze information about the criminal justice system as measured by effective study and research.
Students will be able to demonstrate the ability to think critically and be open to new ideas and perspectives as exhibited in classroom discussions.
Students will be able to demonstrate an awareness of and commitment to ethical behavior in criminal justice as measured by examination and written reports.
Students will use written and verbal communication skills necessary to work in the criminal justice community as measured by the presentation of research papers.

system-page.name: index
system-page.title: Culinary Management, AAS
system-page.summary: The purpose of the culinary management program is to increase the number of individuals trained in specialized food handling and preparation skills to fill a growing number of entry-level positions in the regional foodservice industry.
system-page.teaser: Become familiar with the range of opportunities found in the hospitality field and learn skills for starting positions within various settings of the food service industry.
system-page.keywords: culinary management, culinary, Associate of Applied Science Degree in Culinary Management
system-page.description: Learn skills for starting positions within various settings of the food service industry.

banner.alt: alt text

content.p: The Culinary Management (CLM) program at MCC introduces you to the range of opportunities found in the hospitality field. We prepare you with the necessary skills for starting positions within various settings of the food service industry. Coursework includes an introduction to hospitality and safety and sanitation, as well as skills classes in both culinary skills and baking and pastry. Courses are designed to meet industry standards.

The purpose of the CLM program is to increase the number of individuals trained in specialized food handling and preparation skills to fill a growing number of entry-level positions in the regional food service industry.

Stackable MCC certificates in this program include: Chef's Assistant I Certificate > Chef's Assistant II Certificate > Beverage Management Certificate > Assistant Restaurant Manager Certificate building up to the Culinary Management AAS
Sweet Scots Bakery , our student-run bakery, operates in the Fall semester.
Tartan Bistro , our student-run restaurant, operates in the Spring semester.

div.p: All students in lab skills classes must purchase their own approved MCC student uniform, hat, and tool kit from the MCC Store by the second week of classes. We suggest you pre-order your supplies from the MCC Store online and pick them up from the store, B162. Student course fees only cover the cost of food.
